{"attributes": {"furniture": "page-header"}, "bbox": [0.039074, 0.050467, 0.916369, 0.01631], "canonical_class": "Page-header", "content": {"text": "GLOBAL SUSTAINABILITY REPORT 2024 ABOUT ACCESS AND AFFORDABILITY DATA WORKFORCE ENVIRONMENT QUALITY SUPPLY CHAIN ETHICS AND GOVERNANCE APPENDIX", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 0, "source_label": "header"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.039373, 0.151154, 0.267559, 0.041151], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "We aim to create a strong cybersecurity foundation by reducing cybersecurity risks through:", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 2, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "list-item"}, "bbox": [0.039373, 0.229047, 0.224235, 0.040329], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Building cybersecurity into our systems and applications", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 4, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.039373, 0.426415, 0.281845, 0.245432], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "We have taken several steps to strengthen our internal understanding of cybersecurity, including updating cybersecurity capability training to offer employees a variety of role-based learning paths, including mentorship and certification. Companywide training customized for specific functions within the organization helps employees and contractors better understand company policies and emerging cybersecurity risks. We monitor results to identify retraining requirements and help everyone at Abbott understand their shared responsibility to protect sensitive data and personal information.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 10, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.039373, 0.709356, 0.267619, 0.143292], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Our Medical Device Cybersecurity Council consists of physicians, Chief Information Officers and Chief Information Security Officers, and other healthcare cybersecurity experts, along with key Abbott stakeholders. The Council meets several times a year to advise on Abbott's cybersecurity program and focus areas and offer counsel.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 12, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.039373, 0.867053, 0.270354, 0.102435], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Additionally, Abbott businesses have dedicated cybersecurity leaders to help address cybersecurity risks within their business. These leaders provide updates to leadership and look at how cybersecurity controls are applied across Abbott's businesses.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 13, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.359809, 0.124724, 0.273113, 0.041151], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Regional security leaders in key geographies support local and regional cybersecurity requirements.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 14, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.359809, 0.221946, 0.273424, 0.122864], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "For 137 years, our goal at Abbott has been to help people around the world live fuller, healthier lives. Whether we are building cutting-edge heart devices, tracking new viruses, or helping people stay hydrated, we know that the right combination of science and technology can make lives better.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 16, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.359809, 0.359215, 0.253103, 0.122864], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Technology and data are transforming healthcare, helping us better manage health and wellness, accelerate innovation, tackle business challenges and expand access to care. Perhaps the most transformative tool we are experiencing today is artificial intelligence (AI).", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 17, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.680242, 0.124758, 0.273952, 0.245432], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "AI can harness the power of data in a way that most of us have never seen before. For healthcare, where systems and workers are continually under pressure and time is critical, the potential of AI is immense. We believe AI holds great promise for improving access to care and optimizing areas such as clinical research, product performance, patient care, and productivity. At the same time, the stakes are high, and AI must be managed, applied, and interpreted responsibly and safely, with the same commitment to transparency, scientific standards and excellence our stakeholders expect from us.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 18, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.680242, 0.384595, 0.269934, 0.082007], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "As with all that we do, we believe our use of AI must remain guided by our values — pioneering, achieving, caring, and enduring — and always in service of supporting people and making their lives better.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 19, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"title_level": "paragraph"}, "bbox": [0.383859, 0.554781, 0.360784, 0.018654], "canonical_class": "Section", "content": {"text": "ABBOTT'S APPROACH TO AI IS BUILT ON FIVE KEY PRINCIPLES:", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 20, "source_label": "paragraph_title"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.427991, 0.601054, 0.220688, 0.169862], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "We are focused on improving healthcare around the world and helping people live healthier lives. We will be intentional with our use of AI. We will use AI to deliver meaningful solutions, but we will not sacrifice our principles in pursuit of AI. This means that we will uphold scientific standards, ensure there is human oversight and accountability, and continue to review and determine when it makes sense to deploy AI solutions and when it does not.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 23, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.427991, 0.803679, 0.224367, 0.132277], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "We will not use AI in any way that could negatively impact the quality or safety of any of our products. We will design our AI systems to be safe, secure and protect data and privacy appropriately. We will test AI systems internally and, when an outside perspective would help, with external partners to ensure they are ready to deploy before use.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 26, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.715209, 0.601068, 0.212486, 0.075903], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "We will be transparent about our use of AI and how we use the data people share with us. We will be responsive in communicating with people who have questions about our AI systems or features.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 29, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.715209, 0.709735, 0.22662, 0.094695], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "We will use representative and inclusive data sets whenever possible to inform and train our AI systems. Our goal is to minimize the potential for stereotyping and unfair bias in our AI models, and we will continually work to test, analyze, correct, and improve our systems.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 32, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.715209, 0.837194, 0.221252, 0.113486], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "We believe AI should make life better for humans. AI has the potential to affect all aspects of society, and we will strive to deliver AI solutions to positively impact people. We will leverage AI-enabled technologies and tools to make life-changing products and services more accessible around the world.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 35, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.08400974025974027, 0.42142857142857143, 0.25, 0.02670807453416149], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Improved local air quality and reduced the urban heat island effect.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 9, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "list-item"}, "bbox": [0.3790584415584416, 0.2670807453416149, 0.25446428571428575, 0.05434782608695652], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "* 300 trees and saplings Off-setting 3 tons of carbon emissions, positively impacting more than 10,000 citizens", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 18, "source_label": "list-item"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "list-item"}, "bbox": [0.3790584415584416, 0.43291925465838504, 0.25446428571428575, 0.040372670807453416], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "* Reverse Osmosis (RO) water purifiers provided in two government schools", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 21, "source_label": "list-item"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "list-item"}, "bbox": [0.6745129870129871, 0.25838509316770186, 0.22564935064935068, 0.055590062111801244], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "* Distribution and installation of energy-efficient LEDs, benefiting over 1 lakh rural residents (8 villages)", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 25, "source_label": "list-item"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "list-item"}, "bbox": [0.6745129870129871, 0.47422360248447204, 0.2528409090909091, 0.027639751552795033], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "* Improved travel conditions for 7,000 rural population", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 30, "source_label": "list-item"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.08400974025974027, 0.6282608695652174, 0.8457792207792209, 0.029192546583850933], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "A volunteering opportunity that encourages employees to choose areas based on their interests. Employees are encouraged to volunteer in the community and log-in their efforts at the MySeva Platform.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 32, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.08319805194805197, 0.1372670807453416, 0.8474025974025975, 0.030745341614906833], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Our sustainability policy, founded on four pillars and harmonized with our corporate values, offers directives for achieving the ESG goals that we have established for the business and the entire value chain.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 1, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.11607142857142858, 0.3161490683229814, 0.12053571428571429, 0.04565217391304347], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Our endeavor to have 100% green certified portfolio", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 4, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.5434253246753248, 0.3161490683229814, 0.14691558441558442, 0.04565217391304347], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Our aim of creating a safe, nurturing & inspiring workplace", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 10, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.7800324675324676, 0.3161490683229814, 0.11201298701298702, 0.04565217391304347], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Our commitment to drive a positive change", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 13, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.08319805194805197, 0.47701863354037266, 0.8474025974025975, 0.030745341614906833], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Aligned with our ambitious aim of emerging as a global ESG leader by 2030, we are spearheading bold initiatives. These include achieving net-zero emissions, enhancing water and waste management, and transitioning from greener to Net Zero portfolio.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 15, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "footnote"}, "bbox": [0.08319805194805197, 0.9101863354037266, 0.7069805194805195, 0.01000000000000006], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Notes: (1) GGBS - Ground granulated blast-furnace slag | (2) DBC: Decarbonization Business Charter | (3) MTCoE: Mahindra TERI Centre of Excellence", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 55, "source_label": "text"}

{"bbox": [0.46557673438154223, 0.630217728531856, 0.2790953985648489, 0.012798337950138527], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Vision: Together we Rise for Planet Positive", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 18, "attributes": {}}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "key-value"}, "bbox": [0.10805853866949315, 0.22708646448647107, 0.29515158304354044, 0.037386608588986264], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Date of first appointment as a Director: 29 March 2019\nDate of appointment as Chairperson: 1 July 2021\nLength of service as a Director (as at 28 February 2025): 5 years 11 months", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 3, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.11281959347608614, 0.36198018888147865, 0.341679233457984, 0.10991865948932934], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Ms. Stefanie Yuen Thio is the Joint Managing Partner of TSMP Law Corporation and heads its corporate practice. Admitted to the Singapore Bar in 1994, she has over 31 years of legal experience in mergers and acquisitions, equity capital markets, corporate transactions and regulatory advice. Her clients range from listed corporates to international companies, including in the logistics industry and REITs. She is regularly named by legal journals as a leading practitioner in her areas of specialisation.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 8, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.11264201838795732, 0.47618363543254577, 0.35704548161204264, 0.21893998308879575], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Prior to her appointment, she served on the board of ARA Trust Management (CACHE) Limited, manager of Cache Logistics Trust. Ms. Yuen Thio was appointed by the Monetary Authority of Singapore (MAS) to the Corporate Governance Advisory Committee in 2025, and in 2017 she served on the MAS Corporate Governance Council to review the Code of Corporate Governance. She is a Fellow of the Singapore Institute of Directors. From 2014 to 2017, she was a member of the Expert Panel, Centre for Cross-Border Commercial Law in Asia. She was also a member of the Singapore Governance and Transparency Index Advisory Panel from June 2016 to May 2018. Ms. Yuen Thio established SG Her Empowerment Limited, a women’s empowerment charity with Institute of Public Character status in March 2022 where she is the current chairperson and director of the charity. On February 2025, she was appointed as Singapore’s Alternate Representative to the ASEAN Intergovernmental Commission on Human Rights.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 9, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "list-item"}, "bbox": [0.11432634307116998, 0.7102461056592988, 0.3406465930473514, 0.011285668349847669], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "• Bachelor of Law, National University of Singapore", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 11, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "list-item"}, "bbox": [0.11495627798685215, 0.7209842439977135, 0.32479249535537347, 0.02235315834603654], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "• Advocate and Solicitor of The Supreme Court of Singapore", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 12, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "list-item"}, "bbox": [0.11432659614376907, 0.8073506573932928, 0.3473618744640816, 0.02179407631478654], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "• ASEAN Intergovernmental Commission on Human Rights (AICHR)", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 18, "source_label": "text"}

Ltd. and Acrophyte Hospitality Business Trust Management Pte. Ltd., the managers of Acrophyte Hospitality Trust", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 21, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "key-value"}, "bbox": [0.5251456507240855, 0.22593429937595277, 0.2898534560785061, 0.03028950016673019], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Date of first appointment as a Director: 8 January 2019\nLength of service as a Director (as at 28 February 2025): 6 years 1 month", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 24, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.5295479230182926, 0.37648223132621955, 0.35605897484756105, 0.22581477467606711], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Mr. Ronald Lim has more than 36 years of experience in the banking and finance industry. Mr. Lim was with United Overseas Bank Ltd (UOB) from March 1973 to November 2009 where he last held the appointment of Executive Director and Division Head of Commercial Banking in which the Bank is a leader in the SMEs market. Prior to the above and during his tenure with UOB, Mr. Lim had also held leadership appointments as Head of Human Resource and Head of Branches Division where he was involved in the management and development of human capital, its delivery channels and banking services. From November 2009 to October 2011, Mr. Lim was an adviser to RGE Pte Ltd, a resource-based manufacturing group in the paper and pulp, palm oil and the oil and gas industries.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 29, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "list-item"}, "bbox": [0.530833710461128, 0.6332292063643292, 0.314252989233994, 0.03996206888338421], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "• Bachelor of Social Science, University of Singapore (currently known as the National University of Singapore)", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 31, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.2949894304973323, 0.7935979182545732, 0.20222260265815548, 0.06980575933689025], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Human Development Index (HDI): A composite index measuring average achievement in three basic dimensions of human development — a long and healthy life, knowledge and a decent standard of living. See Technical note 1 at http://hdr.undp.org/sites/default/files/hdr2018_technical_notes.pdf fordetails on how the HDI is calculated.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 24, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.294933, 0.870105, 0.185165, 0.020107], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Life expectancy at birth: Number of years a\nnewborn infant could expect to live if prevailing", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 25, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.5153688309832316, 0.625757490948933, 0.2050117306592988, 0.019215832221798762], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Life expectancy at birth: Number of years a newborn infant could expect to live if prevailing\npatterns of age-specific mortality rates at the time of birth stay the same throughout the infant’s life.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 26, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.5157068764291158, 0.6524942239900915, 0.1976632169397866, 0.048839617473323216], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Expected years of schooling: Number of years of schooling that a child of school entrance age can expect to receive if prevailing patterns of age-specific enrolment rates persist throughout the child’s life.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 27, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.515531631097561, 0.7093217058879573, 0.19545166015624993, 0.039343559451219506], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Mean years of schooling: Average number of years of education received by people ages 25 and older, converted from education attainment levels using official durations of each level.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 28, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.5157583245998475, 0.7644672375190549, 0.2024364638910061, 0.06138910060975604], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Gross national income (GNI) per capita:\nAggregate income of an economy generated by its production and its ownership of factors of production, less the incomes paid for the use of factors of production owned by the rest of the world, converted to international dollars using PPP rates, divided by midyear population.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 29, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.5157742235137195, 0.8328130359184451, 0.19883455602134142, 0.03974055592606703], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "GNI per capita rank minus HDI rank: Difference in ranking by GNI per capita and by HDI value. A negative value means that the country is better ranked by GNI than by HDI value.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 30, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.5152973156440548, 0.8795411942644817, 0.19988334841844518, 0.019316346703506043], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "HDI rank for 2016: Ranking by HDI value for 2016, which was calculated using the same most recently\nrevised data available in 2018 that were used to calculate HDI values for 2017.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 31, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.737744, 0.625307, 0.187512, 0.019976], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "revised data available in 2018 that were used to\ncalculate HDI values for 2017.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 32, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.7373880525914634, 0.6725877119855183, 0.1970954411204268, 0.04929288538490851], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Columns 1 and 7: HDRO calculations based on data from UNDESA (2017a), UNESCO Institute for Statistics (2018), United Nations Statistics Division (2018b), World Bank (2018b), Barro and Lee (2016) and IMF (2018).", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 34, "source_label": "text"}

{"bbox": [0.07172035775533536, 0.6656890124809453, 0.20362069478849087, 0.01782429020579258], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "a See http://hdr.undp.org/en/composite/IHDI for the listof surveys used to estimate inequalities.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 4, "attributes": {"text_role": "footnote"}}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "footnote"}, "bbox": [0.07235709585794589, 0.6871949433117378, 0.19602184016530108, 0.018522830125762224], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "b Based on countries for which an Inequality-adjusted Human Development Index value is calculated.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 5, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "footnote"}, "bbox": [0.07200960484946645, 0.7085725514481708, 0.1956148473227896, 0.017920160060975687], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "c Calculated by HDRO from the 2015–2020 period life tables from UNDESA (2017a).", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 6, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "footnote"}, "bbox": [0.07187760515910824, 0.7299386671112804, 0.19698225812214176, 0.008720167206554948], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "d Data refer to 2017 or the most recent year available.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 7, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "footnote"}, "bbox": [0.07194950010718369, 0.7424110970846037, 0.19211052871331938, 0.017166003715701237], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "e Data refer to the most recent year available during the period specified.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 8, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "footnote"}, "bbox": [0.0719965567239901, 0.7631735899390244, 0.14483716266911206, 0.008260587366615795], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "f Data refer to a year earlier than 2010.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 9, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.07190280076934069, 0.7989314977134148, 0.20216958394864712, 0.06454119426448172], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Human Development Index (HDI): A composite index measuring average achievement in three basic dimensions of human development — a long and healthy life, knowledge and a decent standard of living. See Technical note 1 at http://hdr.undp.org/sites/default/files/hdr2018_technical_notes.pdf for details on how theHDI is calculated.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 11, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.07173607058641387, 0.8715214129192073, 0.19766387939453123, 0.045931962176067175], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Inequality-adjusted HDI (IHDI): HDI value adjusted for inequalities in the three basic dimensions of human development. See Technical note 2 at http://hdr.undp.org/sites/default/files/hdr2018_technical_notes.pdf fordetails on how the IHDI is calculated.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 12, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.07201623684022485, 0.925091642054116, 0.1941988093678544, 0.01762879906631099], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Overall loss: Percentage difference between the IHDI value and the HDI value.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 13, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.2929224526009908, 0.6539927115091464, 0.20059909727515246, 0.02680217463795728], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Difference from HDI rank: Difference in ranks on the IHDI and the HDI, calculated only for countries for which an IHDI value is calculated.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 14, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.29321134241615854, 0.6884527439024389, 0.20413077005525918, 0.01790045017149396], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Coefficient of human inequality: Average inequality in three basic dimensions of human development.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 15, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.29303877667682926, 0.7137973394626524, 0.204375029773247, 0.027500952743902476], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Inequality in life expectancy: Inequality in distribution of expected length of life based on data from life tables estimated using the Atkinson inequality index.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 16, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.2928795195788872, 0.748756669207317, 0.20319993926257626, 0.03634342844893297], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Inequality-adjusted life expectancy index: HDI life expectancy index value adjusted for inequality in distribution of expected length of life based on data from life tables listed in Main data sources.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 17, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.29303207769626527, 0.7927040658346037, 0.2036685403963414, 0.027817561451981734], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Inequality in education: Inequality in distribution of years of schooling based on data from household surveys estimated using the Atkinson inequality index.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 18, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.29307149747522865, 0.8273700100038109, 0.20441552138910057, 0.03636391244283544], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Inequality-adjusted education index: HDI education index value adjusted for inequality in distribution of years of schooling based on data from household surveys listed in Main data sources.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 19, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.29293483827172256, 0.8718763695693598, 0.20066986828315544, 0.026886552019817173], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Inequality in income: Inequality in income distribution based on data from household surveys estimated using the Atkinson inequality index.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 20, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.29314479920922254, 0.9063329482660061, 0.20104533870045732, 0.03620075504954258], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Inequality-adjusted income index: HDI income index value adjusted for inequality in income distribution based on data from household surveys listed in Main data sources.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 21, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.5144942716272866, 0.6538581959794207, 0.18196193788109755, 0.027181962176067033], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Quintile ratio: Ratio of the average income of the richest 20 percent of the population to the average income of the poorest 20 percent of the population.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 22, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.5141915372522866, 0.688377655773628, 0.20316703982469506, 0.036980278201219505], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Palma ratio: Ratio of the richest 10 percent of the population's share of gross national income (GNI) divided by the poorest 40 percent's share. It is based on the work of Palma (2011).", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 23, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.5144829577934451, 0.7324844583650915, 0.1963668302210366, 0.04675269150152445], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Gini coefficient: Measure of the deviation of the distribution of income among individuals or households within a country from a perfectly equal distribution. A value of 0 represents absolute equality, a value of 100 absolute inequality.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 24, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.5146254525533536, 0.8056249999999999, 0.20104313548018296, 0.036855468749999995], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Column 1: HDRO calculations based on data from UNDESA (2017a), UNESCO Institute for Statistics (2018), United Nations Statistics Division (2018b), World Bank (2018b), Barro and Lee (2016) and IMF (2018).", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 26, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.5148220155297255, 0.8500600824123474, 0.19628727610518296, 0.05580429449314025], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Column 2: Calculated as the geometric mean of the values in inequality-adjusted life expectancy index, inequality-adjusted education index and inequality-adjusted income index using the methodology in Technical note 2 (available at http://hdr.undp.org/sites/default/files/hdr2018_technical_notes.pdf).", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 27, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.5147021484375, 0.9130573551829269, 0.20059451219512203, 0.008869509813262084], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Column 3: Calculated based on data in columns 1 and 2.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 28, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.5143277200838415, 0.9283986756859756, 0.19258461556783538, 0.026957293254573075], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Column 4: Calculated based on IHDI values and recalculated HDI ranks for countries for which an IHDI value is calculated.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 29, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.7359473847179878, 0.6537977562881098, 0.2048307688643292, 0.04603241711128049], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Column 5: Calculated as the arithmetic mean of the values in inequality in life expectancy, inequality in education and inequality in income using the methodology in Technical note 2 (available at tp://hdr.undp.org/sites/default/files/hdr2018_technical_notes.pdf).", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 30, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.7358696169969512, 0.7071426019435975, 0.20295422065548782, 0.01802918969131099], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Column 6: Calculated based on abridged life tables from UNDESA (2017a).", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 31, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.7358867663871951, 0.7325391815929878, 0.1732813095464939, 0.017766946932164702], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Column 7: Calculated based on inequality in life expectancy and the HDI life expectancy index.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 32, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.7358513957698171, 0.7574863638528965, 0.20388177639100605, 0.10358273389862802], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Columns 8 and 10: Calculated based on data from the Luxembourg Income Study database, Eurostat's European Union Statistics on Income and Living Conditions, the World Bank's International Income Distribution Database, the Center for Distributive, Labor and Social Studies and the World Bank's Socio-Economic Database for Latin America and the Caribbean, ICF Macro Demographic and Health Surveys and United Nations Children's Fund Multiple Indicator Cluster Surveys using the methodology in Technical note 2 (available at tp://hdr.undp.org/sites/default/files/hdr2018_technical_notes.pdf).", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 33, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.7359445264862805, 0.8682681497713415, 0.19662722703887203, 0.017786239996189012], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Column 9: Calculated based on inequality in education and the HDI education index.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 34, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.7359059999047255, 0.8934263052591465, 0.19168236709222566, 0.01807355182926827], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Column 11: Calculated based on inequality in income and the HDI income index.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 35, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.7361747332317073, 0.9187268959603659, 0.1959195169588415, 0.01747862280868901], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Columns 12 and 13: HDRO calculations based on data from World Bank (2018a).", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 36, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.094537, 0.167, 0.8123738200175592, 0.493], "canonical_class": "Table", "content": {"html": "
OUE BAYFRONT ONE RAFFLES PLACE OUE DOWNTOWN OFFICE
**Property Description** Located at Collyer Quay in Singapore's central business district ("CBD"), OUE Bayfront is a premium Grade A office building which occupies a vantage position between the Marina Bay downtown and established financial hub of Raffles Place. One of the tallest buildings in the Singapore CBD, One Raffles Place is an iconic commercial development comprising two Grade A office towers and a retail mall, strategically located in the heart of main financial district Raffles Place. OUE Downtown Office is part of the OUE Downtown mixed-use development, a refurbished landmark property comprising Grade A offices, a retail podium as well as serviced residences, strategically located in Shenton Way.

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.09483316561070886, 0.15399390243902442, 0.5337192591225229, 0.03951505335365854], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Guided by our Investor Relations Policy, the Manager is committed to maintaining regular, timely and transparent communication with Unitholders, prospective investors, analysts, the media, regulators and other stakeholders.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 2, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.09488197884908538, 0.2050645781726372, 0.5339365591653964, 0.06788428329839942], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Financial results, business updates, announcements, press releases, presentation slides and other relevant disclosures are issued through SGXNet and are posted in a timely manner on OUE REIT's website (https://ouereit.com). Unitholders and other stakeholders can subscribe to email alerts via the website (https://investor.ouereit.com/email_alerts.html) to receive the latest updates on OUE REIT.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 3, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.0946526504144436, 0.30748704863757625, 0.5342769418111661, 0.09562342201791162], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "The AGM is an important communication channel between the board of directors, management of OUE REIT and the Unitholders. OUE REIT convened its 10th AGM on 24 April 2024 in a physical format at Hilton Singapore Orchard, where OUE REIT’s performance, growth strategies and outlook were presented to Unitholders. As specified in the Notice of AGM, Unitholders may submit substantial and relevant questions related to the resolutions to be tabled for approval at the AGM in advance of the AGM.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 5, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.09464872034584602, 0.4156102622427592, 0.5340282756526296, 0.05381737685785063], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "All resolutions were duly passed, and the results were announced on SGXNet and OUE REIT’s website on the same day of the AGM. Detailed minutes of the AGM were also published within one month on SGXNet and OUE REIT’s website on 23 May 2024, in line with best practices.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 6, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.0947913193121189, 0.5035870212461891, 0.5343715760766006, 0.053989615091463466], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "The Manager strives to maintain regular engagement with Unitholders (both retail and institutional) and the wider investment community to provide updates on OUE REIT’s development and financial performance, share insights on its strategies market outlook, and gather feedback for consideration.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 8, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.09478563262195122, 0.5687932307545733, 0.5346593345083841, 0.06891035870807927], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "In FY 2024, the senior management team engaged with more than 300 research analysts and institutional investors through various both in-person and virtual events and activities. These include quarterly analysts and investors briefings. The Manager also hosted one-on-one meetings, group meetings, investor conferences, property tours and non-deal roadshows throughout the year.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 9, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.09444796678496571, 0.648468642816311, 0.5343558632455222, 0.13984488138338422], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Retail investors are a key stakeholder group of OUE REIT. As part of its outreach programme for retail investors, the Manager actively collaborated with the Singapore Exchange (“SGX”), Securities Investors Association (Singapore), REIT Association of Singapore (“REITAS”) and various brokerage firms to connect with retail investors via webinars. The Manager also recognised the importance of in-person engagement and has attended various physical events, including the REITs Symposium jointly organized by ShareInvestor and REITAS in May 2024, which was well-attended by over 1,100 retail participants. In August, the Manager also participated in The Edge REITs Investment Forum’s panel discussion, and shared insights with over 140 participants.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 10, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.09456112745331555, 0.7995890100990855, 0.5345303716892149, 0.06831007050304877], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "To broaden our communications channels, we enhanced media outreach and leverage digital platforms such as OUE REIT’s LinkedIn, Moomoo and Tiger Brokers community pages to provide educational content, including videos, and infographics, to help investors gain a better understanding of OUE REIT, industry and market trends, empowering them to make informed decisions.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 11, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.09428856082078887, 0.8793457626714941, 0.5344358937333271, 0.06839218511814026], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "A testament to the Manager’s investors engagement efforts, OUE REIT is now well-covered by 7 research houses in Singapore and was included in the S&P Global Ex-US Property Index in 2024. The REIT received 5 analyst initiation reports in 2024 from Phillip Securities, Maybank Securities, KGI Securities, Beansprout and OCBC Investment Research, all recommending “BUY” ratings.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 12, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.09352171362900154, 0.13640998654249242, 0.7889324951171874, 0.034210205078125], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "The overview below is based on the International Integrated Reporting Council framework and includes resource inputs, value outcomes and societal impact across various financial and Environmental, Social and Governance (ESG) dimensions.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 2, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "document-index", "furniture": "page-header"}, "bbox": [0.09233667393118075, 0.03925446824121594, 0.8259841992785337, 0.037433074799873116], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Contents | 1 Message from the CEO | 2 Board of Management and Executive Committee | 3 Strategy and Businesses | 4 Financial performance | 5 Environmental, Social and Governance | 6 Risk management\n7 Supervisory Board | 8 Supervisory Board report | 9 Corporate governance | 10 Group financial statements | 11 Company financial statements | 12 ESG statements | 13 Further information", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 0, "source_label": "header"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.08546847459746572, 0.18316226121855947, 0.8365307989353087, 0.10274358981993144], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "“ In 2023, we delivered 6% comparable sales growth <sup>*)</sup>, 320 bps improvement in profitability and an operating cash flow of EUR 2.1 billion, as we improved our supply chain, established a simplified operating model and enhanced patient safety and quality.”", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 2, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "list-item"}, "bbox": [0.0940711789014863, 0.42175072646722567, 0.39215756300019056, 0.07141577743902446], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "• Sales amounted to EUR 18.2 billion, an increase of 2% on a nominal basis. On a comparable basis<sup>)</sup>, sales increased 6%, driven by supply chain improvements. Comparable sales<sup>)</sup> showed 11% growth in the Diagnosis & Treatment segment, 1% growth in the Connected Care segment, and 3% growth in the Personal Health segment.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 6, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "list-item"}, "bbox": [0.09418165997761054, 0.4972403475133385, 0.3939981525700267, 0.07031669802782019], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "• Net income amounted to a loss of EUR 463 million, driven by higher earnings offset by a EUR 575 million Respironics litigation provision. Net income for 2022 was a loss of EUR 1.6 billion, which included a charge of EUR 1.5 billion related to goodwill and R&D impairments.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 7, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "list-item"}, "bbox": [0.09423319746808309, 0.5727038276486281, 0.39522296440310595, 0.08936487709603656], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "• As of December 31, 2023, over 99% of the sleep therapy device registrations that are actionable had been remediated, while the remediation of the ventilator devices remains ongoing. In October 2023, Philips Respironics received preliminary court approval for the class action settlement that would resolve all or nearly all private economic loss claims in the US.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 8, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "list-item"}, "bbox": [0.09461139213748095, 0.6665705983231709, 0.39219310481373854, 0.053586842606707395], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "• Adjusted EBITA<sup>)</sup> amounted to EUR 1,921 million, or 10.6% of sales, compared to 7.4% of sales in 2022. All segments showed an increase in Adjusted EBITA<sup>)</sup> margin, mainly driven by increased sales and pricing & productivity measures, partly offset by cost inflation.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 9, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "list-item"}, "bbox": [0.0946185898199314, 0.7237053996760673, 0.38657993223609, 0.052717225609756106], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "• Supported by significant change management efforts, by year-end 2023 Philips had reduced its workforce by approximately 8,000 roles, out of 10,000 roles in total planned by 2025.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 10, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "list-item"}, "bbox": [0.09427345089796112, 0.778450481135671, 0.38638787246331935, 0.035767077934451164], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "• Net cash flows from operating activities amounted to EUR 2,136 million; free cash flow<sup>*)</sup> amounted to EUR 1,582 million.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 11, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "list-item"}, "bbox": [0.09442923941263337, 0.8183262076028964, 0.3909825841391959, 0.03362822741996953], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "• Philips cancelled approximately 15.1 million shares acquired under its 2021 share buyback program for capital reduction purposes.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 12, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.08268508176787237, 0.093530942712854, 0.4185346465816634, 0.11543153525635053], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Another large strategic investment in 2018 in Extruded Solutions was breaking ground for the construction of a new high-performance extrusion press at the Cressona plant in Pennsylvania in the U.S. The new press will have state-of-the-art capabilities primarily targeted at meeting the needs of the growing automotive market in North America. The amount of aluminium content per vehicle in the U.S. automotive industry is projected to increase as OEMs are demanding stronger, light-weight applications that help", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 1, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"title_level": "section-header"}, "bbox": [0.08573302478325076, 0.2362425582401663, 0.692179691965987, 0.011677631594056277], "canonical_class": "Section", "content": {"text": "Our strategy is about “value over volume” driven by innovation, collaboration and simplification", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 3, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.08367977900407478, 0.26241675668813813, 0.8549057835142951, 0.17177100748499707], "canonical_class": "Table", "content": {"html": "

", "type": "table"}, "ro_index": 4, "source_label": "table"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.08215407953029726, 0.4898892137480945, 0.41451974264005337, 0.13958427615282012], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Extruded Solutions is the leading global supplier of extrusion-based aluminium solutions with a market share of 20 percent in Europe and 23 percent in North America. The business area also has a solid foothold in emerging markets with extrusion capacity in South America and Asia. Extruded Solutions has four business units: Extrusion Europe, Extrusion North America, Precision Tubing and Building Systems. The business units are responsible for their respective value chains, from casthouses, aluminium extrusion and value-adding operations to commercial activities such as product development and sales.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 6, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.08226752304449314, 0.6435476014672258, 0.4123294365115283, 0.06099782059832307], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Extruded Solutions has an extensive network of production plants that ensures global reach combined with local presence. During 2018, Extruded Solutions focused on simplification, and adding more value and locating resources closer to customers.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 7, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.08239005484232088, 0.7337198099275916, 0.3969004784560785, 0.0624326529153963], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Extruded Solutions is a margin business and the LME aluminium cost element is passed on to the customer. Contracts are typically short to medium term. Extruded Solutions will continue to shift its portfolio toward higher-margin products.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 9, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.082, 0.8239999999999998, 0.408, 0.023006755764429426], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "• Strong technology competence with dedicated R&D centers and more than 1,000 engineers close to customers", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 11, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.082, 0.8504890051936607, 0.408, 0.024715723841439774], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "• Global reach and local presence, increasing flexibility and reliability for customers", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 12, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.082, 0.8769985066959374, 0.408, 0.011023482916740879], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "• Speed in delivery and proximity to customers", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 13, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.082, 0.8906497550034045, 0.408, 0.02300675576442937], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "• Value chain breadth and depth, from extrusion to surface finishing and fabrication, welding and assembly", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 14, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.082, 0.91628427615856, 0.408, 0.012752947302367215], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "• Strong and agile product development capabilities", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 15, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.082, 0.9308105048131482, 0.408, 0.010189495186851638], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "• Technical leadership in precision tubing and extrusion", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 16, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.5225931307164634, 0.4898300245331555, 0.4169980230564025, 0.08805393125952747], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Extruded Solutions’ innovation model promotes fast decision paths from idea to product. The business area has four R&D centers, located in Finspång (Sweden), Troy (USA), Karmøy (Norway) and Toulouse (France). These are complemented by application centers covering nearly 40 locations across the four business units and by corrosion labs in China, the U.S. and Norway.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 18, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.5223772151295731, 0.6655184117759148, 0.3962554782774391, 0.03742455459222555], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "All efforts are shared across business units to achieve optimal knowledge transfer and to enable faster, better impact in other markets where similar opportunities arise.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 21, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.5223847775342988, 0.7168742258955793, 0.4173597084603658, 0.07600044064405495], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "To deliver better products to the customers faster, and to address specific challenges, Extruded Solutions collaborates with leading universities such as the Norwegian University of Science & Technology, Michigan Technological University, Massachusetts Institute of Technology and the University of Oxford, as well as with specialized companies.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 22, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.5224262814405488, 0.8069396079458842, 0.4184541730182927, 0.06038068073551828], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Building Systems has opened physical showrooms in selected cities in Europe to promote their latest product developments. Product innovation is vital to success in that market, mainly in Europe, where end-users are becoming advanced.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 23, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.5224397389481708, 0.8837838224085368, 0.4129407036013719, 0.04978896722560975], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "In Precision Tubing, the innovation is focused on developing new aluminium applications for the automotive industry, such as fuel and brake lines. Aluminium substitutes copper and steel, resulting in comparable performance, but lighter.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 24, "source_label": "text"}

{"bbox": [0.5233, 0.0931, 0.4145, 0.11536345211551556], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "improve fuel economy and payload. The new press in Cressona will be able to produce new higher strength alloys for applications that need additional value-added fabrication and will provide Extruded Solutions in North America with a solid platform for future business development. The plant has sound financial performance, an established automotive culture, and vast technical resources to successfully deliver on this expansion project and capture new business opportunities in North America.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 2, "attributes": {}}

For example, the equations in the preceding subequations environment were Eqs. (6).", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 5, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.517549423589939, 0.1922389035108613, 0.40099460508765244, 0.04113401831650151], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "The equation that follows is set in a wide format, i.e., it spans the full page. The wide format is reserved for long equations that cannot easily be set in a single column:", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 7, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.08796499857088415, 0.3452135932736281, 0.4006824028201219, 0.05609363090701215], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "This is typed to show how the output appears in wide format. (Incidentally, since there is no blank line between the equation environment above and the start of this paragraph, this paragraph is not indented.)", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 9, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.5178941382431403, 0.39212935284870426, 0.400591356230945, 0.04196902391387198], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Figures and tables are usually allowed to \"float\", which means that their placement is determined by \\LaTeX, while the document is being typeset.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 18, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.08799696015148628, 0.4975550805068597, 0.40073044195407775, 0.07055783155487809], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "REVTEX will automatically number such things as sections, footnotes, equations, figure captions, and table captions. In order to reference them in text, use the \\label{#1} and \\ref{#1} commands. To reference a particular page, use the \\pageref{#1} command.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 11, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.5179268292682927, 0.43738066882621945, 0.4002748666158536, 0.09922732469512198], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Use the figure environment for a figure, the table environment for a table. In each case, use the \\caption command within to give the text of the figure or table caption along with the \\label command to provide a key for referring to this figure or table. The typical content of a figure is an image of some kind; that of a table is an alignment.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 19, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.08786663520626906, 0.575277724847561, 0.40068316948123095, 0.0848000428734757], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "The \\label{#1} should appear within the section heading, within the footnote text, within the equation, or within the table or figure caption. The \\ref{#1} command is used in text at the point where the reference is to be displayed. Some examples: Section I on page 1, Table I, and Fig. 1.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 12, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.5179464200647865, 0.5399750500190549, 0.40043189072027435, 0.08458043540396346], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Insert an image using either the graphics or graphix packages, which define the \\includegraphics{#1} command. (The two packages differ in respect of the optional arguments used to specify the orientation, scaling, and translation of the image.) To create an alignment, use the tabular environment.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 20, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.518026093273628, 0.627882705316311, 0.40028469178734766, 0.07037889434070124], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "The best place to locate the figure or table environment is immediately following its first reference in text; this sample document illustrates this practice for Fig. 1, which shows a figure that is small enough to fit in a single column.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 21, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.5335259265434451, 0.7016014434070122, 0.3841592035060976, 0.012458317454268269], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "In exceptional cases, you will need to move the float", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 22, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "caption"}, "bbox": [0.08796872022675305, 0.7071012766768293, 0.4003501333841463, 0.1042868116425305], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "TABLE I. A table that fits into a single column of a two-column layout. Note that REVTEX 4 adjusts the intercolumn spacing so that the table fills the entire width of the column. Table captions are numbered automatically. This table illustrates left-, center-, decimal- and right-aligned columns, along with the use of the ruledtabular environment which sets the Scotch (double) rules above and below the alignment, per APS style.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 13, "source_label": "caption"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "caption"}, "bbox": [0.5175547232278964, 0.8849802901105183, 0.40058266244283536, 0.02403623999618901], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "FIG. 1. A figure caption. The figure captions are automatically numbered.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 24, "source_label": "caption"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "caption"}, "bbox": [0.16304102455697408, 0.1842425537109375, 0.6797884461937881, 0.011039250071455804], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "FIG. 2. Use the figure* environment to get a wide figure that spans the page in twocolumn formatting.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 2, "source_label": "caption"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "caption"}, "bbox": [0.08747145489948552, 0.22457203934832318, 0.8308078300662157, 0.02430754870903201], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "TABLE II. This is a wide table that spans the full page width in a two-column layout. It is formatted using the table* environment. It also demonstates the use of \\multicolumn in rows with entries that span more than one column.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 3, "source_label": "caption"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "footnote"}, "bbox": [0.090678, 0.361125, 0.280028, 0.017562], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "<sup>a</sup> The $z$ parameter of these positions is $z \\sim \\frac{1}{4}$.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 5, "source_label": "footnote"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.0879376816168064, 0.4049039812785824, 0.40005953160727886, 0.04141979682736278], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "earlier in the document, as was done with Table II: LATEX’s float placement algorithms need to know about a full-page-width float earlier.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 6, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.08769880341320503, 0.4492522151295732, 0.4007490874499809, 0.02682977443788104], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Fig. 2 has content that is too wide for a single column, so the figure* environment has been used.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 7, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.08803667021960745, 0.4790538657583841, 0.40047148169540775, 0.2295281535823171], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "The content of a table is typically a tabular environment, giving rows of type in aligned columns. Column entries separated by &’s, and each row ends with \\\\. The required argument for the tabular environment specifies how data are aligned in the columns. For instance, entries may be centered, left-justified, right-justified, aligned on a decimal point. Extra column-spacing may be be specified as well, although REVTEX 4 sets this spacing so that the columns fill the width of the table. Horizontal rules are typeset using the \\hline command. The doubled (or Scotch) rules that appear at the top and bottom of a table can be achieved enclosing the tabular environment within a ruledtabular environment. Rows whose columns span multiple columns can be typeset using the \\multicolumn{#1}{#2}{#3} command (for example, see the first row of Table II).", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 8, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "caption"}, "bbox": [0.08785893137862043, 0.7473470846036585, 0.400549629025343, 0.06395489948551841], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "TABLE III. Numbers in columns Three–Five are aligned with the “d” column specifier (requires the dcolumn package). Non-numeric entries (those entries without a “.”) in a “d” column are aligned on the decimal point. Use the “D” specifier for more complex layouts.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 9, "source_label": "caption"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "footnote"}, "bbox": [0.08982243979849466, 0.9001053377477134, 0.2668211625262005, 0.009724657012195053], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "<sup>b</sup> Some tables need more than one footnote.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 12, "source_label": "footnote"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.5178279820884146, 0.4050278082126525, 0.4006357183689024, 0.15710103849085363], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Tables I, II, III, and IV show various effects. A table that fits in a single column employs the table environment. Table II is a wide table, set with the table* environment. Long tables may need to break across pages. The most straightforward way to accomplish this is to specify the [H] float placement on the table or table* environment. However, the LATEX 2ε package longtable allows headers and footers to be specified for each page of the table. A simple example of the use of longtable can be found in the file summary.tex that is included with the REVTEX 4 distribution.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 13, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.5342055902248475, 0.566924483136433, 0.38433849799923786, 0.012255561642530494], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "There are two methods for setting footnotes within a", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 14, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "caption"}, "bbox": [0.5176669683689025, 0.620295469702744, 0.40111602038871946, 0.050526748285060985], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "TABLE IV. A table with numerous columns that still fits into a single column. Here, several entries share the same footnote. Inspect the LATEX input for this table to see exactly how it is done.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 15, "source_label": "caption"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.03035879925983708, 0.27808456793064024, 0.21614598530094797, 0.096257919683689], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "We believe transparent ESG reporting is a business imperative, which is why we focus on the metrics that matter most to our stakeholders.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 1, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.03064959456280964, 0.39368875643102136, 0.20990414317061262, 0.1575249797541921], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "We welcome the standardization, simplification and harmonization of international reporting standards, and we are committed to driving meaningful impact and transparently disclosing our progress. Our approach to disclosure is informed by global standards and frameworks including those outlined below, and we continue monitoring and preparing for the evolving reporting landscape.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 2, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.2801007824409299, 0.22017323563738564, 0.188912948980564, 0.17533445776962656], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "We publish disclosures referencing the GRI standards, emphasizing the issues and indicators that are most relevant to our business and our stakeholders. GRI standards enhance the comparability and quality of information on economic, environmental and social impacts (positive and negative) organizations have. They also create a common language to communicate that information to stakeholders.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 5, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.28011733636623476, 0.5011397794397866, 0.19353640672637196, 0.15453518006859762], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "We map our disclosures to the standards for software and IT services and hardware established by SASB. These standards help to guide the reporting of ESG issues most pertinent to stakeholders. We are disclosing on the topics that we consider material to our operations, using SASB’s standards for the hardware and software and IT industries.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 7, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.51864990234375, 0.18412295457793446, 0.1967147603849085, 0.09760847882526677], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Dell, alongside 25 other companies, was one of the initial signatories of the initiative by the WEF to align and focus corporate ESG disclosures. To meet this commitment, Dell reports on the WEF framework’s core Stakeholder Capitalism Metrics.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 9, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"title_level": "section-header"}, "bbox": [0.5191995760289634, 0.40258089391196644, 0.1563668897675305, 0.03632133669969512], "canonical_class": "Section", "content": {"text": "CDP Water Security and Climate Change Responses", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 11, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.5188013290777439, 0.4561592213700457, 0.1777082936356708, 0.07637930521150912], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Since 2017, we have provided <u>CDP Water Security and Climate Change responses</u>, which help us to measure, manage and report our environmental impact.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 12, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.5189622237042683, 0.6878241115663108, 0.19407542158917684, 0.0571932760099086], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "In the <u>Goals Dashboard</u>, we map alignment of our goals with the U.N. Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs).", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 15, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.7453928282202744, 0.1831527040062881, 0.22276075409679874, 0.21762140041444358], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "ESG reporting is rapidly evolving with the growing adoption of the International Sustainability Standards Board’s (ISSB) reporting standards globally, transposition of the Corporate Sustainability Reporting Directive (CSRD) in European Union member countries and development of other regional and national requirements around the world. Dell currently reports on ESG in its financial statements; however, these new or evolving regulations require expanded reporting. We are actively preparing for these comprehensive and granular disclosures covering a broad range of sustainability topics.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 17, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.7621962487215491, 0.45761256124996147, 0.1714112538353528, 0.03846673241086011], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Our GRI, SASB, and WEF SCM indexes can be found in our <u>Reporting Frameworks Index</u>.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 18, "source_label": "form"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "document-index"}, "bbox": [0.7621962487215491, 0.5108653579463157, 0.1691923830325723, 0.0384667324108601], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "View current and archived copies of all our <u>ESG reports.</u>", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 19, "source_label": "form"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.03067800292775208, 0.9295150533536585, 0.6914513016321648, 0.04960231427316654], "canonical_class": "Page-footer", "content": {"text": "<u>INTRODUCTION</u>

{"attributes": {"text_role": "caption"}, "bbox": [0.06101473552424735, 0.06835076308831936, 0.21619268277796302, 0.009861747927782015], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Table 5 | Payment and deferral of variable remuneration", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 0, "source_label": "caption"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.06101149396198552, 0.188638633169779, 0.3025328324480755, 0.10197368342701982], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "We pay no severance if an employee voluntarily resigns or is dismissed for malpractice. Severance payments to daily policymakers as defined in the Wet op het financieel toezicht (Wft; Dutch Financial Supervision Act) are capped at 100% of fixed remuneration. No severance would be paid to daily policymakers if they are dismissed due to a failure of Robeco (for example, in the event of a request for state aid or if substantial sanctions are imposed by the regulator).", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 3, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"title_level": "paragraph"}, "bbox": [0.06122010766006098, 0.30802954697027446, 0.2640888790967988, 0.01293957221798781], "canonical_class": "Section", "content": {"text": "Rules for Identified Staff and Control Function Staff", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 4, "source_label": "paragraph_title"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.06104949206840702, 0.3244707507621951, 0.2915344684879955, 0.011724526009908517], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Additional rules apply to Identified Staff and Control Function Staff.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 5, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.061139522645531644, 0.3702101098037348, 0.2953430361864044, 0.14800224490282013], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Identified Staff are employees who can have a material impact on Robeco’s risk profile and/or the funds we manage. Identified Staff include the Management Board, ExCo members, senior portfolio management staff, the heads of control functions (such as Compliance, Risk Management and Internal Audit) and other risk-takers as defined in the Alternative Investment Fund Managers Directive (AIFMD) and the Undertakings for Collective Investment in Transferable Securities Directive V (UCITS V) whose total remuneration places them in the same remuneration bracket as the other staff members we refer to.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 7, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.06087610012147485, 0.5369908179306403, 0.3014706569764673, 0.10214117282774389], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Additional rules that apply to Identified Staff include part of their variable remuneration being paid in cash and part of it being deferred and converted into R-CARs, as set out in the payment/redemption table below. The threshold of EUR 100,000 does not apply to these staff members. In the occasional event that variable remuneration is more than twice the amount of fixed remuneration, the percentages in brackets in the table below apply.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 8, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "caption"}, "bbox": [0.06139604894126335, 0.6593383193597562, 0.2857864491532489, 0.009837140339176785], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Table 6 | Payment and deferral of variable remuneration of Identified Staff", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 9, "source_label": "caption"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.06098084240424924, 0.8098472632431404, 0.30291879793492765, 0.04174649866615852], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Control Function Staff are employees who work in our Compliance, Risk Management and Internal Audit departments. The following rules apply to the fixed and variable remuneration of Control Function Staff.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 12, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "list-item"}, "bbox": [0.060890034001048025, 0.8700112542873476, 0.2960823542897295, 0.026755132907774454], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "The fixed remuneration is sufficient to guarantee that Robeco can attract qualified and experienced staff.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 13, "source_label": "list-item"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "list-item"}, "bbox": [0.39419085246760677, 0.06659239978325078, 0.2981455137671495, 0.026960508765243897], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "The KPIs of Control Function Staff are predominantly role-specific and non-financial.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 14, "source_label": "list-item"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "list-item"}, "bbox": [0.39424152653391775, 0.09728077213938645, 0.302422500238186, 0.02701794880192454], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "The financial KPIs are not based on the financial results of the part of the business that the employee covers in their monitoring role.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 15, "source_label": "list-item"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "list-item"}, "bbox": [0.39433364495998485, 0.12789708579458844, 0.3025545743616617, 0.026306092797256098], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "The KPIs may not be based on the financial results of the business part they oversee in their monitoring role.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 16, "source_label": "list-item"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "list-item"}, "bbox": [0.3943925959889482, 0.1582532333746189, 0.2983224264005336, 0.040893733326981714], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "The rules above apply in addition to the rules that apply to Identified Staff if an employee is part of both the Control Function Staff and Identified Staff.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 17, "source_label": "list-item"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "list-item"}, "bbox": [0.39507878001143304, 0.20332395972275155, 0.2971292635289635, 0.05657680604516007], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "The remuneration of the Head of Compliance, Head of Internal Audit and the Head of Risk fall under the direct supervision of the Supervisory Board, as advised by the Nomination & Remuneration Committee.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 18, "source_label": "list-item"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.3946723454173019, 0.2946768411775915, 0.30011519269245435, 0.07191013243140242], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Robeco has set out clear risk control procedures to prevent and address remuneration-related risks. These include an assessment of possible risks, an annual remuneration policy review process and shareholder approval of our remuneration policy. We elaborate on these aspects below.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 20, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.3946418278391769, 0.4004634801352897, 0.28263511099466465, 0.026912633384146366], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Robeco has identified the following risks that must be taken into account in applying its remuneration policy:", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 22, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "list-item"}, "bbox": [0.3942938976753049, 0.44594687857278964, 0.3033006026105184, 0.07176707197980187], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "misconduct or a serious error of judgement on the part of employees (such as taking non-permitted risks, violating compliance guidelines or exhibiting behavior that conflicts with our core values) in order to meet business objectives or other objectives;", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 23, "source_label": "list-item"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "list-item"}, "bbox": [0.39424194335937507, 0.5217604920922256, 0.25897428186928356, 0.011645865091463394], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "a considerable deterioration in Robeco’s financial results;", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 24, "source_label": "list-item"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "list-item"}, "bbox": [0.39397151295731714, 0.536470322027439, 0.23540616663490854, 0.011350990853658588], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "a serious violation of the risk management system;", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 25, "source_label": "list-item"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "list-item"}, "bbox": [0.39422881335746957, 0.5522968869092989, 0.29961741377667694, 0.011244402629573144], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "evidence that fraudulent acts have been committed by employees;", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 26, "source_label": "list-item"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.39442933617568604, 0.5974144316882622, 0.30196500452553354, 0.026623832888719508], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "The following risk control measures apply, all of which are monitored by the Supervisory Board.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 28, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.3945706400057165, 0.6580005478277441, 0.29335428377477135, 0.026481754954268272], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Robeco may reclaim all or part of the variable remuneration paid to an employee if:", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 30, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "list-item"}, "bbox": [0.39422399009146347, 0.7035989305449695, 0.2768366520579268, 0.011275009527439012], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "the payment was made on the basis of incorrect information;", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 31, "source_label": "list-item"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "list-item"}, "bbox": [0.3943538609946647, 0.7187026009908538, 0.24163794540777447, 0.011356350038109754], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "it becomes clear that the employee committed fraud;", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 32, "source_label": "list-item"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "list-item"}, "bbox": [0.39436618711890253, 0.7340270102896342, 0.295518233136433, 0.02646555830792679], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "they have engaged in serious improper behavior or demonstrated serious negligence in the performance of their tasks;", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 33, "source_label": "list-item"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "code"}, "bbox": [0.090003, 0.458381, 0.225576, 0.165221], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Algorithm 1 Bubble Sort
Input: data $x_i$, size $m$
repeat
Initialize $noChange = true$.
for $i = 1$ to $m - 1$ do
if $x_i > x_{i+1}$ then
Swap $x_i$ and $x_{i+1}$
$noChange = false$
end if
end for
until $noChange$ is $true$", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 3, "source_label": "code"}
