The table title should be set in 9 point type and centered unless it runs two or more lines, in which case it should be flush left.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 11, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.5008430592606707, 0.3573648890053353, 0.38431854992378056, 0.088287353515625], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Tables contain textual material, whereas figures contain graphical material. Specify the contents of each row and column in the table’s topmost row. Again, you may float tables to a column’s top or bottom, and set wide tables across both columns. Place two-column tables at the top or bottom of the page.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 12, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.5007981612042683, 0.4880361566310976, 0.3864178615663109, 0.04027629573170728], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "The preferred way is to number definitions, propositions, lemmas, etc. consecutively, within sections, as shown below.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 14, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.501260897008384, 0.5387291587271341, 0.3841349680830793, 0.028411001810213464], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Definition 2.1. A function $f : X \\rightarrow Y$ is injective if for any $x, y \\in X$ different, $f(x) \\neq f(y)$.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 15, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.5014045231516768, 0.5820426233803353, 0.38466046589176833, 0.012729313548018338], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Using Definition 2.1 we immediate get the following result:", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 16, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.5007201552972561, 0.6021446265243903, 0.38594601514862803, 0.04368646389100618], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Proposition 2.2. If $f$ is injective mapping a set $X$ to another set $Y$, the cardinality of $Y$ is at least as large as that of $X$", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 17, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.501, 0.667, 0.383, 0.013], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Proof. Left as an exercise to the reader. $\\square$", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 18, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.5013387242759146, 0.7015139696074695, 0.3029991187118902, 0.013333889100609751], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Lemma 2.3 stated next will prove to be useful.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 19, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.5000210199123475, 0.7217304806592987, 0.385242354230183, 0.028607743426067176], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Lemma 2.3. For any $f : X \\rightarrow Y$ and $g : Y \\rightarrow Z$ injective functions, $f \\circ g$ is injective.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 20, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.5005699194931403, 0.7577823099275914, 0.3867129144435975, 0.026530702172256043], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Theorem 2.4. If $f : X \\rightarrow Y$ is bijective, the cardinality of $X$ and $Y$ are the same.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 21, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.5009267220846035, 0.8010162204649389, 0.33110321789253055, 0.01301251667301827], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "An easy corollary of Theorem 2.4 is the following:", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 22, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.501320741234756, 0.8214639505525915, 0.38586902153201225, 0.02807200362042678], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Corollary 2.5. If $f : X \\rightarrow Y$ is bijective, the cardinality of $X$ is at least as large as that of $Y$.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 23, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.5006954435022866, 0.8778945550685976, 0.38378918159298775, 0.028012397580030494], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Remark 2.7. According to some, it is only the finite case (cf. Assumption 2.6) that is interesting.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 25, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "list-item"}, "bbox": [0.07103610675039247, 0.6625302663438256, 0.20172684458398746, 0.048426150121065374], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "a Data collection methods, age ranges, sampled women (ever-partnered, ever-married or all women) and definitions of the forms of violence and of perpetrators vary by survey. Thus data are not necessarily comparable across countries.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 4, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "list-item"}, "bbox": [0.07103610675039247, 0.7124697336561744, 0.18210361067503925, 0.020278450363196126], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "b Data refer to the most recent year available during the period specified.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 5, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "list-item"}, "bbox": [0.07103610675039247, 0.7342615012106537, 0.20172684458398746, 0.07657384987893462], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "c Data refer to people recognized as refugees under the 1951 UN Convention, the 1967 UN Protocol and the 1969 Organization of African Unity Convention. In the absence of government figures, the Office of the UN High Commissioner for Refugees (UNHCR) has estimated the refugee population in many industrial countries based on 10 years of individual asylum-seeker recognition.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 6, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "list-item"}, "bbox": [0.07103610675039247, 0.8123486682808717, 0.17935635792778654, 0.019975786924939468], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "d For more detailed country notes, see www.prisonstudies.org.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 7, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "list-item"}, "bbox": [0.07103610675039247, 0.8338377723970944, 0.20486656200941916, 0.009987893462469734], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "e HDRO calculations based on data from ICPS (2016).", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 8, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "list-item"}, "bbox": [0.07103610675039247, 0.8453389830508474, 0.1978021978021978, 0.019975786924939468], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "f Differs from standard definition or refers to only part of the country.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 9, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "list-item"}, "bbox": [0.07103610675039247, 0.8668280871670703, 0.20486656200941916, 0.010593220338983052], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "g Refers to years or periods other than those specified.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 10, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "list-item"}, "bbox": [0.07103610675039247, 0.8789346246973365, 0.20172684458398746, 0.048728813559322036], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "h Refers to Palestinian refugees under the UNHCR mandate only. Another 5,589,488 Palestinian refugees are under the responsibility of United Nations Relief and Works Agency for Palestinian Refugees in the Near East.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 11, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "list-item"}, "bbox": [0.07103610675039247, 0.9291767554479419, 0.1558084772370487, 0.009987893462469734], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "i May include citizens of South Sudan.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 12, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "list-item"}, "bbox": [0.29238618524332816, 0.6501210653753027, 0.18759811616954475, 0.020278450363196126], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "j Refers to the average for 2009–2011, prior to South Sudan's independence.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 13, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "list-item"}, "bbox": [0.29238618524332816, 0.6719128329297821, 0.20408163265306123, 0.02875302663438257], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "k An unknown number of refugees and asylum-seekers from South Sudan may be included under data for Sudan.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 14, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.29238618524332816, 0.7381961259079903, 0.20525902668759813, 0.048426150121065374], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "**Birth registration:** Percentage of children under age 5 who were registered at the moment of the survey. It includes children whose birth certificate was seen by the interviewer and children whose mother or caretaker says the birth has been registered.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 17, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.29238618524332816, 0.7917675544794188, 0.20525902668759813, 0.05780871670702179], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "**Refugees by country of origin:** Number of people who have fled their country of origin because of a well founded fear of persecution due to their race, religion, nationality, political opinion or membership in a particular social group and who cannot or do not want to return to their country of origin.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 18, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.29238618524332816, 0.8547215496368038, 0.20525902668759813, 0.07627118644067797], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "**Internally displaced persons:** Number of people who have been forced to leave their homes or places of habitual residence—in particular, as a result of or to avoid the effects of armed conflict, situations of generalized violence, violations of human rights or natural or human-made disasters—and who have not crossed an internationally recognized state border.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 19, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.5137362637362637, 0.6501210653753027, 0.20486656200941916, 0.06779661016949153], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "**Homeless people due to natural disaster:** Average annual number of people who lack a shelter for living quarters as a result of natural disasters, who carry their few possessions with them and who sleep in the streets, in doorways or on piers, or in any other space, on a more or less random basis, expressed per million people.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 20, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.5137362637362637, 0.7230629539951574, 0.20486656200941916, 0.019975786924939468], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "**Orphaned children:** Number of children (ages 0–17) who have lost one or both parents due to any cause.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 21, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.5137362637362637, 0.7481840193704601, 0.19505494505494508, 0.029963680387409198], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "**Prison population:** Number of adult and juvenile prisoners—including pretrial detainees, unless otherwise noted—expressed per 100,000 people.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 22, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.5137362637362637, 0.7832929782082325, 0.20290423861852436, 0.029661016949152543], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "**Homicide rate:** Number of unlawful deaths purposefully inflicted on a person by another person, expressed per 100,000 people.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 23, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.5137362637362637, 0.8180992736077481, 0.19976452119309265, 0.029661016949152543], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "**Suicide rate:** Number of deaths from purposely self-inflicted injuries, expressed per 100,000 people in the reference population.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 24, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.5137362637362637, 0.8529055690072639, 0.20486656200941916, 0.05750605326876514], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "**Justification of wife beating:** Percentage of women and men ages 15–49 who consider a husband to be justified in hitting or beating his wife for at least one of the following reasons: if his wife burns the food, argues with him, goes out without telling him, neglects the children or refuses sexual relations.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 25, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.5137362637362637, 0.9155569007263922, 0.18956043956043958, 0.020278450363196126], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "**Violence against women ever experienced, intimate partner:** Percentage of the female", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 26, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.7354788069073783, 0.6501210653753027, 0.18485086342229198, 0.029963680387409198], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "population, ages 15 and older, that has ever experienced physical or sexual violence from an intimate partner.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 27, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.7354788069073783, 0.685230024213075, 0.18759811616954475, 0.048728813559322036], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "**Violence against women ever experienced, nonintimate partner:** Percentage of the female population, ages 15 and older, that has ever experienced sexual violence from a nonintimate partner.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 28, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.7354788069073783, 0.7391041162227603, 0.1989795918367347, 0.029963680387409198], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "**Depth of the food deficit:** Number of kilocalories needed to lift the undernourished from their status, holding all other factors constant.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 29, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "list-item"}, "bbox": [0.08263525331087254, 0.15264030642625762, 0.26154718166444363, 0.10468793848205879], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "a The results refer to the situation as captured by the latest available survey and thus to the situation in the year indicated in Column 1. Not all indicators were available for all countries, so caution should be used in cross-country comparisons. When an indicator is missing, weights of available indicators are adjusted to total 100 percent. See Technical note at https://hdr.undp.org/sites/default/files/publications/additional-files/2025-10/2025_gMPI_TechnicalNote_1.pdf andOPHI Methodological Note 61athttps://ophi.org.uk/publications/MN-61 for details.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 2, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "list-item"}, "bbox": [0.08358693302441654, 0.25795096448714094, 0.25247587317521947, 0.08331426628656126], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "b D indicates data from Demographic and Health Surveys, M indicates data from Multiple Indicator Cluster Surveys, N indicates data from national surveys and P indicates data from Pan Arab Population and Family Health Surveys (see https://hdr.undp.org/mpi-2025-faqs andOPHIMethodological Note 61athttps://ophi.org.uk/publications/MN-61 for the list ofnational surveys).", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 3, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "list-item"}, "bbox": [0.08153104456459605, 0.3466185927972561, 0.2501518882193217, 0.018137802496189008], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "c Data refer to the most recent year available during the period specified.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 4, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "list-item"}, "bbox": [0.08154520174352134, 0.381720595941311, 0.25410081816882624, 0.017630525914634133], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "e Value is not reported because it is based on a small number of multidimensionally poor people.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 6, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "list-item"}, "bbox": [0.08127073706650154, 0.416796279535061, 0.25318170314881866, 0.025542855611661607], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "g Considers child deaths that occurred at any time because the survey did not collect the date of child deaths.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 8, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "list-item"}, "bbox": [0.08034033798589939, 0.4731230945121951, 0.2636837842987805, 0.02740311785442074], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "j Captures only deaths of children under age 5 who died in the last five years and deaths of children ages 12–18 years who died in the last two years.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 11, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "list-item"}, "bbox": [0.08108827172256097, 0.5048872189405488, 0.2595864495998476, 0.035793814310213466], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "k The methodology was adjusted to account for missing indicator on nutrition and incomplete indicator on child mortality (the survey did not collect the date of child deaths).", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 12, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "list-item"}, "bbox": [0.08095200701457698, 0.5454012242759146, 0.2458095271412919, 0.016747332317073215], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "l Based on the version of data accessed on 7 June 2016.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 13, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "list-item"}, "bbox": [0.08092162341606325, 0.5819097156059451, 0.2630315194478849, 0.05402230611661586], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "n Given the information available in the data, child mortality was constructed based on deaths that occurred between surveys—that is, between 2012 and 2014. Child deaths reported by an adult man in the household were taken into account because the date of death was reported.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 15, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "list-item"}, "bbox": [0.0810444529463605, 0.6410944050114329, 0.24872617395912727, 0.01733708079268297], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "o Revised estimate from the 2022 MPI based on the survey microdata update.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 16, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "list-item"}, "bbox": [0.08116539187547638, 0.6756536418635671, 0.2590893777986852, 0.036612280868902475], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "q Child mortality data were not used because the data were collected from a sample of women ages 15–49 that was not representative of the female population in that age group.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 18, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "list-item"}, "bbox": [0.08096896287871569, 0.7156931211890245, 0.2622844789086319, 0.054372320407774316], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "r Anthropometric data were collected from all children under age 5 and from selected individuals who are age 5 or older. Construction of the nutrition indicator was restricted to children under age 5 since the anthropometric sample is representative of the under 5 population.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 19, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "list-item"}, "bbox": [0.08124368062833459, 0.7753059498856707, 0.21790129126572028, 0.02678365567835371], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "s Indicator on sanitation follows the national classification in which pit latrine with slab is considered unimproved.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 20, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "list-item"}, "bbox": [0.08106390976324314, 0.8063564095846036, 0.26047941626572024, 0.017515422541920744], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "t Following the national report, latrines are considered improved for the sanitation indicator.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 21, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "list-item"}, "bbox": [0.08110638130001906, 0.8284392863948171, 0.2568664476348133, 0.02718523723323173], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "u Indicator on sanitation follows the national classification in which flush to \"don't know where\" is considered unimproved.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 22, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "list-item"}, "bbox": [0.08086417593607088, 0.8595455411585367, 0.26076465885813643, 0.04564381669207307], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "v The analytical sample was restricted to the Multiple Indicator Cluster Survey sample, and its sample weight was used, because child mortality information was not collected for the National Immunization Coverage Survey sample.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 23, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "list-item"}, "bbox": [0.36935019888528964, 0.15258565763147866, 0.2645876107564787, 0.06364420076695884], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "w Because of the high proportion of children excluded from nutrition indicators due to measurements not being taken, estimates based on the 2019 Serbia Multiple Indicator Cluster Survey should be interpreted with caution. The unweighted sample size used for the multidimensional poverty calculation is 82.8 percent.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 24, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "list-item"}, "bbox": [0.3695043349847561, 0.23409715605945122, 0.2660889862804878, 0.025716284775152438], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "y The analytical sample was restricted to the round 2 sample because standard of living questions were not collected for the round 1 sample.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 26, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.37065021794016767, 0.28706123165967984, 0.24838316382431402, 0.02595342868711892], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Multidimensional Poverty Index: Proportion of the population that is multidimensionally poor adjusted by the intensity of the deprivations.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 28, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.37068865520198174, 0.3231416730182927, 0.2549159798971037, 0.05244914729420728], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Multidimensional poverty headcount: Population with a deprivation score of at least 33.3 percent. It is expressed as a share of the population in the survey year, the number of multidimensionally poor people in the survey year and the projected number of multidimensionally poor people in 2023.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 29, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.3705473513719512, 0.38688419993330797, 0.23248815024771338, 0.02597489519817067], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Intensity of deprivation of multidimensional poverty: Average deprivation score experienced by people in multidimensional poverty.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 30, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.37071854754192074, 0.4232362626238567, 0.2588461675876524, 0.05332665420160062], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Inequality among the poor: Variance of individual deprivation scores of poor people. It is calculated by subtracting the deprivation score of each multidimensionally poor person from the intensity, squaring the differences and dividing the sum of the weighted squares by the number of multidimensionally poor people.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 31, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.3706735303925305, 0.48693782155106713, 0.2611993854801829, 0.026076779249237774], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Population in severe multidimensional poverty: Percentage of the population in severe multidimensional poverty—that is, those with a deprivation score of 50 percent or more.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 32, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.37086253096417676, 0.5229409417873476, 0.2407327196074695, 0.03480802210365852], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Population vulnerable to multidimensional poverty: Percentage of the population at risk of suffering multiple deprivations—that is, those with a deprivation score of 20–33.3 percent.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 33, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.370688446789253, 0.5683183474657013, 0.2475565393959604, 0.03473221941692074], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Contribution of deprivation in dimension to overall multidimensional poverty: Percentage of the Multidimensional Poverty Index attributed to deprivations in each dimension.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 34, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.37049387862042676, 0.6141365758384146, 0.25252119855182925, 0.05361518673780488], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Population living below national poverty line: Percentage of the population living below the national poverty line, which is the poverty line deemed appropriate for a country by its authorities. National estimates are based on population-weighted subgroup estimates from household surveys.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 35, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.37064265553544207, 0.6778756192835367, 0.25298938286013717, 0.02606784727515233], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Population living below PPP $3.00 a day: Percentage of the population living below the international poverty line of $3.00 (in 2021 purchasing power parity [PPP] terms) a day.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 36, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.6612235613567073, 0.16531846953601373, 0.2290866758765243, 0.026549250905106698], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Column 1: Refers to the year and the survey whose data were used to calculate the country’s Multidimensional Poverty Index value and its components.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 38, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.661082733898628, 0.20171455197217986, 0.23861137576219504, 0.08169489570055652], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Columns 2–12: HDRO and OPHI calculations based on data on household deprivations in health, education, and standard of living from various surveys listed in column 1 using the methodology described in Technical note (https://hdr.undp.org/sites/default/files/publications/additional-files/2025-10/2025_gMPI_TechnicalNote_1.pdf)andOPHI Methodological Note 61athttps://ophi.org.uk/publications/MN-61 for details.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 39, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.6606567978277439, 0.29273788824314023, 0.2374745736471037, 0.04408884932355185], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Columns 4 and 5:It also uses population data from the United Nations Department of Economic and Social Affairs. 2024.World Population Prospects: The 2024 Revision.New York. https://population.un.org/wpp/.Accessed 11 June 2025.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 40, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.6612352324695122, 0.3475658239268047, 0.22099466463414646, 0.026614305449695123], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Columns 13 and 14: World Bank (2025). World Development Indicators database. Washington, DC.http://data.worldbank.org. Accessed June 11 2025.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 41, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "caption"}, "bbox": [0.08681773860280106, 0.2915438321741616, 0.3915699209818026, 0.0758944181116616], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Figure 1: A document page (left panel, from [Scholz et al., ICML 2014]) decomposed into a set of classified regions (right panel). Body text regions are shown as filled boxes, captions and figure text regions as box outlines, and graphical element regions as dashed box outlines.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 1, "source_label": "caption"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.08690848745950838, 0.41951293945312496, 0.391854047077458, 0.12915423137385673], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Region identification decomposes document pages into regions, each one labelled as either caption, graphical element, body text, or figure text. Caption regions are built by starting from the caption phrases found in the prior step, and combining them with subsequent lines of text. The rest of the text in the document is grouped into paragraphs using Poppler’s paragraph grouping mechanism. Paragraphs that are either too large or aligned to the left margin of a column are classified as body text, otherwise they are classified as figure text.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 3, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.08652136081602516, 0.5511756264291159, 0.3921171383741425, 0.07750738376524388], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Page headers and page numbers are handled as special cases. PDFFigures checks if pages in the document are consistently headed by the same phrase, and if so marks those phrases as body text. Likewise page numbers are detected by checking if all pages end with a number, and if so marking those numbers as body text.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 4, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.08682218225990854, 0.6306024914253049, 0.39197402581935975, 0.07779975705030488], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Finally, the graphical elements of the document are located. To do this each page is rendered as a 2D image using a customized PDF renderer that ignores text. The bounding boxes of the connected components in the resulting image are then used as graphical regions of the document. An example of such a decomposition is shown in Figure 1.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 5, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.08695226901915015, 0.7379513624237805, 0.39157359797780106, 0.1302824885670732], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "The last step is to assign each caption a region of the document containing the figure it refers to. First, up to four “proposal” regions are generated for each caption. Proposal regions are built by generating a rectangular region adjacent to each side of the caption, and then maximally expanding those regions as long as they do not overflow the page margin, overlap with body text, or overlap a caption. This is shown in Figure 2. For two-column papers regions are constrained to not cross the center of the page unless the caption itself spans both columns.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 7, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.08692988698075457, 0.8702341368140245, 0.3913281696598704, 0.037961961699695054], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Next, a single proposed figure region is selected for each caption. To do this a scoring function is used to rate each proposed region based on how likely it is to contain a figure.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 8, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "caption"}, "bbox": [0.5166229992378049, 0.20677085318216465, 0.39125053591844505, 0.07860449814214943], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Figure 2: Generating possible figure regions for a caption. For each caption (center blue box), up to four regions are generated as possible figures by maximally expanding boxes that are adjacent to one side of the caption. Page from [Cuong et al., NIPS 2013]", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 10, "source_label": "caption"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.5160868426067073, 0.3097072396627287, 0.3924016887385669, 0.11680211509146343], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "The scoring function gives higher scores to regions that are large and contain graphical elements. To ensure captions are not assigned regions that overlap, they iterate through every possible permutation of how figure regions could be matched to captions. Each permutation is scored based on the sum of the scores of the proposed regions it includes, with regions that overlap given a score of 0. The highest scoring permutation is then selected as the final set of figure regions to return.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 11, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.5162496427210366, 0.4283009301162347, 0.3918773223132622, 0.11783896841653964], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "An additional complication comes from cases where figures are immediately adjacent, so that they are not separated by any intervening body text or captions. In these cases, proposal regions might get overly expanded and therefore contain multiple figures. To handle these cases, when iterating through permutations, if two proposal regions overlap an attempt is made to split them by detecting a central band of whitespace that separates them. An example of such a figure can be found in Figure 4, second row right column.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 12, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.5161853325076219, 0.5801722084603659, 0.39201380287728665, 0.06480069788490858], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Our approach builds upon [5] by making crucial updates to its important components. Most of the updates are designed to allow PDFFigures 2.0 to generalize across a wider variety of paper formats. The PDFBox [1] library is used for parsing PDFs.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 14, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.5157066977896341, 0.6726921565358233, 0.39888255049542687, 0.18323266006097552], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "We improve the keyword search of [5] that identifies phrases that might start captions to be effective against more kinds of papers by using a considerably expanded set of keywords. However, naively increasing the number of keywords also increases the number of false positives for each paper. We resolve this problem by adding a number of additional filters to the ones used in [5], such as (I): Select phrases that are all caps. (II): Select phrases that are abbreviated. (III): Select phrases that occupy a single line. (IV): Select phrases that do not use the most commonly used font in the document. (V): Select phrases that are left aligned to the text beneath them. The last filter serves as a general purpose filter for detecting indented paragraphs or bullet points that start by mentioning a figure.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 16, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.5166224037728658, 0.8834744783727134, 0.39166974323551834, 0.024616461032774314], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Our region identification method decomposes each page into caption, body text, figure text and graphical regions as", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 18, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"furniture": "page-header"}, "bbox": [0.029413, 0.037873, 0.9412, 0.016605], "canonical_class": "Page-header", "content": {"text": "Contents Introduction Environmental Initiatives Engagement and Advocacy Data Green Bond Impact Report Appendix\n2025 Environmental Progress Report\n15", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 0, "source_label": "header"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.029413, 0.131634, 0.219787, 0.076209], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "We're making progress toward our goal of sourcing only recycled and renewable materials for our products: In 2024, 24 percent of the materials contained in products shipped came from recycled or renewable sources.<sup>28</sup>", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 2, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.268383, 0.096465, 0.221251, 0.135033], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "We've focused our efforts on 15 priority materials based on a broad range of environmental, social, and supply chain impacts. Each are outlined in detail in our Material Impact Profiles white paper.<sup>30</sup> Our priority materials consist of aluminum, cobalt, copper, glass, gold, lithium, paper, plastics, rare earth elements, steel, tantalum, tin, titanium, tungsten, and zinc, and they account for 87 percent of the total product mass shipped to our customers in 2024.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 17, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.268383, 0.253328, 0.223852, 0.291895], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Maintaining our standards for recycled and renewable materials is essential to our journey to create a circular supply chain. Our requirements are based on international standards for recycled content and responsible resource management. By requiring certification to these standards, we're able to confirm that a material has been recycled or comes from a renewable source — one that can continually produce without depleting the earth's natural resources. We approach materials from new sources with the same rigor, evaluating each one for the safety of the material's chemistry. This process allows us to scale our use of materials that are better for the environment and safer for use in our products. Recycled material is certified by third parties to a recycled content standard that conforms with ISO 14021. Total recycled content numbers also include supplier-reported recycled content checked by Apple but not third-party certified.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 18, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.268383, 0.567053, 0.215411, 0.135034], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Our teams are overcoming obstacles to creating closed loop supply chains, including material performance and traceability. This is possible through our work with a diverse group of partners stretching from suppliers to metallurgists to product designers. For example, we were able to design an alloy that meets our rigorous design performance standards and contains 100 percent recycled aluminum.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 19, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.268383, 0.723916, 0.212879, 0.15464], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Barriers to our progress remain — including challenges within our control and those outside our direct influence. Addressing these requires a collective response. Through collaboration across the value chain, we can achieve impact felt beyond our business. The supply chains we're helping innovate serve more than just our product needs — they help promote the availability of competitively priced, quality recycled and renewable materials across geographies.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 20, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.04412, 0.259826, 0.192774, 0.056562], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Goals\nTransition the following to 100 percent recycled by the end of 2025:<sup>29</sup>", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 3, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"title_level": "document"}, "bbox": [0.569852, 0.119647, 0.290267, 0.028464], "canonical_class": "Section", "content": {"text": "Key challenges to developing circular supply chains", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 21, "source_label": "paragraph_title"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.571689, 0.261221, 0.162652, 0.174249], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "While many recycled or renewable materials have indistinguishable technical properties, some may differ from the conventional material. This needs to be accounted for during product design and manufacturing. For example, select recycled plastics differ in properties from other plastics. The composition of other recycled materials can also be impacted by some level of contamination during the recycling process.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 24, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.779437, 0.261221, 0.1453, 0.056602], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Information about the source of materials — whether mined, recycled, or renewable — might not be readily available.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 27, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.779437, 0.416902, 0.154921, 0.095818], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Materials for a single component can come from hundreds of different suppliers, requiring exponentially more effort as we scale the use of high-quality recycled or renewable materials across components and products.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 30, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.571689, 0.534549, 0.171076, 0.174249], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "The supply of recycled and renewable materials can be constrained by the limited availability of recoverable material or production of renewable content. When supply exists in some locations around the world, new suppliers need to be incorporated into supply chains for the material to be accessed. Technological limitations in recovering materials from complex waste streams can pose challenges to efficiency and effectiveness.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 33, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.779408, 0.611799, 0.161094, 0.233072], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Transboundary movement regulations — created to establish critically important community and environmental protections — can have the unintended consequence of inhibiting material recovery and movement to recyclers or refiners for use in new products. Apple supports the U.S. ratification of the Basel Convention to better support recycling and recovery with this key geography. To learn about our support of other policies that enable circular supply chains while improving social and environmental protections, read the Apple 2030 policy platform.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 36, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.571689, 0.808218, 0.172809, 0.056602], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Recycled or renewable content may not be easily accessible on the market, requiring the development of new supply chains.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 39, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"furniture": "page-header"}, "bbox": [0.029413, 0.037873, 0.941202, 0.016605], "canonical_class": "Page-header", "content": {"text": "Contents Introduction Environmental Initiatives Engagement and Advocacy Data Green Bond Impact Report Appendix\n2025 Environmental Progress Report\n49", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 0, "source_label": "header"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.029413, 0.131634, 0.22291, 0.135033], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Water is a local resource. For this reason, we have a context-based strategy that takes into account local conditions where we and our suppliers operate. We collect and analyze data and site-level feedback to understand our water impacts. We also gain insights into local watershed health — such as baseline water stress — using tools like the World Resources Institute (WRI) Aqueduct Water Risk Atlas.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 2, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.268383, 0.096465, 0.225831, 0.056601], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "We've identified that 70 percent of our corporate water use occurs in areas with high basin stress.<sup>57</sup> And we've found that the majority of our water-related impact is in the manufacturing supply chain.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 3, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.268383, 0.174897, 0.223729, 0.095817], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "We know that we can't address water stewardship challenges alone. We believe we must go beyond our operations to collaborate with communities and work in basins on stewardship, replenishment, and WASH (water access, sanitation, and hygiene) projects throughout our value chain.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 4, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.04412, 0.329535, 0.175797, 0.056562], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Goal\nReplenish all our corporate freshwater withdrawals in high-stress locations by 2030.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 5, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"caption": "true", "picture_type": "bar_chart"}, "bbox": [0.042102, 0.419086, 0.196071, 0.069711], "canonical_class": "Picture", "content": {"text": "[Bar chart showing freshwater replenishment progress: 2024 at 40%, 2030 Target at 100%]", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 7}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.04412, 0.518669, 0.180671, 0.056562], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Goal\nCertify all Apple-owned data centers to the Alliance for Water Stewardship (AWS) Standard by 2025.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 8, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"caption": "true", "picture_type": "bar_chart"}, "bbox": [0.043766, 0.608869, 0.193456, 0.070226], "canonical_class": "Picture", "content": {"text": "[Bar chart showing AWS certification progress: 2024 at 7, 2025 Target at 8]", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 10}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.044355, 0.707235, 0.190819, 0.095778], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Goal\nIncrease supplier participation in the Supplier Clean Water Program, prioritizing locations with high water stress and supporting participants in achieving a 50 percent average water reuse rate by 2030.<sup>56</sup>", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 11, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"caption": "true", "picture_type": "bar_chart"}, "bbox": [0.043677, 0.838342, 0.195974, 0.067518], "canonical_class": "Picture", "content": {"text": "[Bar chart showing water reuse rate progress: 2024 at 42%, 2030 Target at 50%]", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 13}

{"attributes": {"title_level": "document"}, "bbox": [0.283089, 0.326534, 0.147414, 0.052974], "canonical_class": "Section", "content": {"text": "We focus our work on five strategic pillars:", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 14, "source_label": "paragraph_title"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.316177, 0.418376, 0.162455, 0.056562], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Low-water design\nMinimizing water impacts in the design of products, Apple-managed services, and sites<sup>58</sup>", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 16, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.316177, 0.508053, 0.136878, 0.056562], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Site efficiency and conservation\nImproving the performance of existing sites and processes", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 18, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.316177, 0.596215, 0.155787, 0.056562], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Site water stewardship\nDemonstrating responsibility beyond our facilities through watershed-level management", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 20, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.316177, 0.684299, 0.149342, 0.056562], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Replenishment and nature-based solutions\nImproving water availability, quality, and access through regenerative approaches", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 22, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.316177, 0.772382, 0.14514, 0.056562], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Leadership and advocacy\nAdvancing water management through policy, advocacy, and technology innovation", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 24, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.283089, 0.861842, 0.170503, 0.015212], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "To learn more, read our white paper, Apple's Water Strategy.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 25, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.507338, 0.131801, 0.218555, 0.115425], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "We approach low-water design by focusing first on site selection whenever possible and conducting a water risk evaluation to determine whether a potential site is in a water-stressed area. The results inform our decision-making and help mitigate the impact of our expected water use. We then design solutions to manage the quality of the wastewater that we return to the watershed.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 27, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.507338, 0.269056, 0.223739, 0.174248], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Our low-water design efforts avoided 161 million gallons of freshwater use in 2024 in our corporate offices and data centers.<sup>59</sup> At our campus in Austin, Texas, we aim to achieve net-zero water use by relying on an onsite wastewater reuse system, as well as condensate recovery and stormwater capture, limiting potable water use to potable purposes only. This effort will save up to 60 million gallons of freshwater use annually.<sup>60</sup> Using wastewater for cooling will also allow us to achieve 28 million kilowatt-hours in energy savings annually at the location.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 28, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.507338, 0.465134, 0.221912, 0.154641], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Our three most recent data centers have prioritized water efficiency by using high-efficiency air-cooled chillers instead of water-cooled systems, resulting in zero water use for cooling. At our newest data center in Waukee, Iowa, we're also pursuing a 221-acre wetland restoration project on the property. The initiative will restore the land to native prairie potholes and wetlands, providing stormwater capture, groundwater recharge, and water quality benefits, as well as creating a habitat for native species.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 29, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.746307, 0.096632, 0.224745, 0.291895], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "In our supply chain, the majority of water is used in manufacturing. We look for opportunities to innovate around water- and chemistry-intensive processes when possible. An example of this is a green anodizing project we initiated, which involved subject matter experts across internal design, product development, manufacturing, and quality teams, as well as an outside vendor piloting acid recovery equipment. The project integrated an acid recovery technology that separated and recycled two process chemistries — sulfuric and phosphoric acids — along with modular reverse osmosis systems that filtered water in the anodizing baths. The team worked to confirm that the approach functioned within both the tight process tolerances and the delicate chemistry required to maintain product quality. From 2020 to 2022, the collaboration facilitated this technology transfer — from early offline experiments and a proof of concept pilot to full production of a MacBook product.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 30, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.746307, 0.410358, 0.212999, 0.056601], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "The impact was profound, resulting in a 75 percent reduction in water usage and approximately 90 percent reduction in chemical use, which also helped achieve carbon savings.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 31, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"furniture": "page-header"}, "bbox": [0.029413, 0.037873, 0.941199, 0.016605], "canonical_class": "Page-header", "content": {"text": "Contents Introduction Environmental Initiatives Engagement and Advocacy Data Green Bond Impact Report Appendix\n2025 Environmental Progress Report\n5", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 0, "source_label": "header"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.043917, 0.247236, 0.253929, 0.056601], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Apple 2030 is our science-based commitment to achieving carbon neutrality for our entire carbon footprint, including transitioning our entire value chain to 100 percent clean electricity.<sup>5</sup>", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 3, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.04412, 0.337771, 0.101102, 0.036993], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Become carbon neutral for our corporate operations.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 4, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.04412, 0.382506, 0.075448, 0.026111], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Read more on our approach to carbon neutrality", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 5, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"caption": "true", "picture_type": "chart"}, "bbox": [0.1663, 0.3399, 0.1508, 0.0867], "canonical_class": "Picture", "content": {"text": "[Timeline showing carbon neutrality achieved in 2020 and maintained through 2024]", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 6, "source_label": "chart"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.04412, 0.454085, 0.108412, 0.095817], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Achieve carbon neutrality for our entire carbon footprint by 2030 — reducing related greenhouse gas emissions by 75 percent compared with 2015.<sup>6</sup>", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 7, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"caption": "true", "picture_type": "chart"}, "bbox": [0.1671, 0.4587, 0.1536, 0.0669], "canonical_class": "Picture", "content": {"text": "[Bar chart showing 60% emissions reduction in 2024 towards 75% target by 2030]", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 9, "source_label": "chart"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.36397, 0.247236, 0.253972, 0.036993], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "We're committed to one day using only recycled or renewable materials in our products and packaging, and to enhance material recovery.<sup>7</sup>", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 11, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.364038, 0.320741, 0.112829, 0.095817], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Transition to 100 percent recycled cobalt, tin, gold, and rare earth elements in select components and applications by the end of 2025.<sup>8</sup>", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 12, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "list-item"}, "bbox": [0.364038, 0.438388, 0.095779, 0.056601], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "· Gold plating in all Apple-designed rigid and flexible printed circuit boards", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 13, "source_label": "list_item"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.681781, 0.31807, 0.102559, 0.056601], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Replenish all of our corporate freshwater withdrawals in high-stress locations by 2030.<sup>11</sup>", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 31, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.681475, 0.382412, 0.1213, 0.013039], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Read more about our water strategy and progress", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 32, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.681475, 0.50402, 0.1213, 0.013039], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Read more about our water strategy and progress", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 35, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.682011, 0.677719, 0.1213, 0.013039], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Read more about our water strategy and progress", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 38, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.681781, 0.731972, 0.105811, 0.056601], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Eliminate waste sent to landfill from our corporate facilities and our suppliers.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 40, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.681475, 0.796315, 0.104242, 0.026111], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Read more about our Zero Waste Program and progress", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 41, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.10825599484327365, 0.13740765827458082, 0.32605055646198555, 0.010710568311737792], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "We begin by evaluating the entire Lloyd’s market.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 2, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.10822834294016768, 0.15472325766958844, 0.397520692406631, 0.03309589665110519], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Our first screen involves analysing the historical underwriting performance of every Lloyd’s syndicate across multiple market cycles.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 3, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.10815194478849087, 0.19426713152629577, 0.3870095453029726, 0.04414708579458843], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "This ensures we identify syndicates with a long-term track record of underwriting profitability, particularly those that have performed well during soft or stressed market environments.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 4, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.10822762838224086, 0.24597849776105188, 0.3516304425495427, 0.03355808165015244], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "This step serves as a quantitative triage, allowing us to focus only on syndicates with proven underwriting capability.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 5, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.10837860107421875, 0.31561481754954274, 0.375348324659394, 0.045158453220274425], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Each syndicate undergoes a comprehensive evaluation, encompassing business plan review, historical and projected performance analysis, and assessment of its impact on the overall portfolio.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 8, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.10837788651629192, 0.3675491556307165, 0.35169214016053735, 0.04456197599085364], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Our analytical toolkit, including stochastic modelling and scenario analysis, enables us to make informed, risk-adjusted decisions within the context of evolving market dynamics.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 9, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.10854092481659681, 0.43094812904916163, 0.39354213063309834, 0.021926180211509137], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "In addition to quantitative evaluation, we conduct in-depth qualitative assessments.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 11, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.10839140357040777, 0.459683272198933, 0.37273962253477516, 0.0219775390625], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "We evaluate underwriting leadership, strategic direction and corporate governance standards.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 12, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.10838661752096036, 0.48829583889100614, 0.34491428282202746, 0.022187678639481734], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Qualitative insight is used to refine and validate the selections identified through our data-driven triage.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 13, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.109524, 0.551811, 0.796005, 0.063227], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "At the heart of Helios’ portfolio optimisation is a whole-of-market approach grounded in deep research and analysis, qualitative judgement, and robust cycle management.”", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 15, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.10856626929306401, 0.6361563929115854, 0.3610760051448171, 0.021640982278963397], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Each syndicate is assessed through a comprehensive, multi-factor scoring framework that includes:", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 17, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.10865068389148247, 0.7685721941692073, 0.38556964409060596, 0.021267089843750003], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Syndicates are graded, and only those in the top quartiles are considered for inclusion.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 25, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.10861668284346418, 0.7973364257812501, 0.3822506118402248, 0.022443966749237775], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "This system provides an objective basis for ranking performance and guides our capital allocation decisions.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 26, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.1087286749118712, 0.843555878429878, 0.3515867130930831, 0.033704625571646434], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "We actively seek to partner with high-performing syndicates, including those that typically do not offer capacity to third party capital providers.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 28, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.10844090903677593, 0.8841394340701221, 0.37487005466368134, 0.031974085365853715], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Our goal is to secure exclusive access for Helios and to position ourselves as a long-term, value-adding partner of choice.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 29, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.1085067823456555, 0.923933820026677, 0.38492401867378045, 0.022569431211890253], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "By aligning ourselves with leading underwriting talent, we enhance our ability to deliver superior returns sustainably.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 30, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.5273998427972562, 0.13727512266577743, 0.39928984851371946, 0.07915554139672257], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "We optimise our portfolio by selecting high quality Lloyd’s syndicates with strong track records, with an objective to build a balanced, diversified portfolio that enhances return on capital while maintaining disciplined exposure to risks. As part of our portfolio construction, we apply an allocation framework that sets maximum capacity limits by the age of each syndicate, avoiding overexposure to any syndicate.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 32, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.5276752453315549, 0.22283983184070125, 0.3642924090129573, 0.033448188595655494], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Our portfolio is designed to broadly mirror Lloyd’s class of business mix, ensuring diversification and mitigating systemic or class-specific risk.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 33, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.5276062904916159, 0.26301078982469517, 0.40098382717225606, 0.04487438667111281], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "However, we selectively overweight outperforming segments and underweight areas facing challenges, creating a smart, risk-aware tilt that enhances performance potential.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 34, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.5274375952743903, 0.3143477872522867, 0.4014953315548781, 0.06639996784489327], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "We are developing an efficient frontier model, a data-driven framework to model the trade-off between risk and return across our syndicates. This approach allows us to construct a portfolio that maximises expected return for Helios’ defined risk appetite and tolerance, forming our strategic allocation baseline.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 35, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.5275630001905488, 0.4058546112804879, 0.3574498618521341, 0.026885331316692036], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Selected syndicates are subject to ongoing oversight.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 37, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "list-item"}, "bbox": [0.5276626214748475, 0.44132720202934456, 0.34815322503810975, 0.022116907631478715], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "quarterly underwriting and claims reviews against business plans; and", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 39, "source_label": "list_item"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "list-item"}, "bbox": [0.5273691763528964, 0.4671598406535824, 0.3842617425685975, 0.02167599561737802], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "regular engagement with managing agents and active underwriters.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 40, "source_label": "list_item"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.5275272722942074, 0.4960919933784299, 0.3961111375762195, 0.010719768245045742], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Resilience through the cycle is fundamental to our strategy.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 41, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "list-item"}, "bbox": [0.5273639362614331, 0.642105802210366, 0.30065560689786586, 0.021517542397103573], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "tactical pre-emptions in hardening market conditions; and", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 43, "source_label": "list_item"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "list-item"}, "bbox": [0.5272781893102134, 0.667894912347561, 0.3756562619092988, 0.02073367235137198], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "strong rate adequacy discipline in challenging market conditions.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 44, "source_label": "list_item"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.5268701171875, 0.6966472346608233, 0.38910078839557943, 0.03362965653582322], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "We monitor rate momentum and pricing adequacy at the risk code level to ensure our portfolio remains dynamically aligned with evolving market conditions.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 45, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.5274870188643294, 0.7550015482088415, 0.3743248023056403, 0.02167629334984753], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Risk management is integral to how we select, build and monitor our Lloyd’s portfolio.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 47, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "list-item"}, "bbox": [0.5273810261051829, 0.8006417325647867, 0.37857493330792685, 0.021905190072408518], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "diversification across classes of business, geographies and perils;", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 49, "source_label": "list_item"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "list-item"}, "bbox": [0.5275668111661586, 0.8265352872522869, 0.35332739853277445, 0.02129412395198159], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "stress and scenario testing of portfolio resilience to adverse events;", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 50, "source_label": "list_item"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "list-item"}, "bbox": [0.5275957507621951, 0.8523177877286586, 0.3777852277057926, 0.021795267244664702], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "ongoing capital adequacy review relative to projected exposures; and", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 51, "source_label": "list_item"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "list-item"}, "bbox": [0.5278512052210366, 0.8782602300876526, 0.3618537419016769, 0.02182641006097555], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "monitoring of syndicate-specific developments and Lloyd’s market-wide trends.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 52, "source_label": "list_item"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.5270616782583841, 0.9068025914634147, 0.39051775676448175, 0.03387713176448174], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Our focus is on ensuring that our portfolio remains resilient through market volatility while concentrating capital in the most compelling underwriting opportunities.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 53, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"furniture": "page-footer"}, "bbox": [0.6540995379192073, 0.9475200076219513, 0.27802096541809096, 0.016953958650914702], "canonical_class": "Page-footer", "content": {"text": "Helios Underwriting plc\nAnnual report and financial statements 2024\n17", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 54, "source_label": "footer"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.4318830322220153, 0.0072231139646869984, 0.12873719500838143, 0.049118323346317605], "canonical_class": "Page-header", "content": {"text": "Value Creation Story\n—Future Mobility Society Envisioned by an\nAutomobile Manufacturer", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 3, "source_label": "page-header"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.03257142857142857, 0.07825040128410915, 0.8597142857142857, 0.04173354735152488], "canonical_class": "Page-header", "content": {"text": "Message from the CSO | Roundtable Discussion among Outside Officers | Dialogues with Shareholders and Investors | Corporate Governance | Risk Management and Compliance | Message from the CFO | Capital Strategies\nEnvironmental Initiatives (Disclosure Based on TCFD Recommendations) | Human Resource Development | Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion | Health and Safety and Social Contribution Activities | Respect for Human Rights\nValue Chain Collaboration | Vehicle Safety | Quality and Information Security | Intellectual Property and Privacy", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 7, "source_label": "page-header"}

{"attributes": {"title_level": "section-header"}, "bbox": [0.03257142857142857, 0.22632423756019263, 0.43657142857142855, 0.019662921348314606], "canonical_class": "Section", "content": {"text": "Members of the Board of Directors and Audit & Supervisory Board Members", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 9, "source_label": "section-header"}

{"attributes": {"title_level": "section-header"}, "bbox": [0.03257142857142857, 0.25601926163723915, 0.1957142857142857, 0.01524879614767255], "canonical_class": "Section", "content": {"text": "#### Board of Directors and Related Structures", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 10, "source_label": "section-header"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.03257142857142857, 0.27247191011235955, 0.43657142857142855, 0.025682182985553772], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Members of the Board of Directors are selected based on comprehensive consideration of their responsibilities to ensure sustainable growth of Toyota and to enhance its corporate value over the medium to long term.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 11, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.03257142857142857, 0.29935794542536115, 0.4468571428571428, 0.052568218298555375], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Toyota believes that it is critical to appoint individuals who are capable of practicing product-centered and region-centered management and contributing to decision-making aimed at sustainable growth in accordance with the Toyoda Principles, which describe the Company's founding philosophy. Moreover, these individuals should be able to play significant roles in transforming Toyota into a mobility company in the areas of electrification, intelligence, diversification, and the formation of partnerships. They must also possess the ability to contribute to the resolution of social issues, such as those pertaining to climate change.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 12, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.03257142857142857, 0.35313001605136435, 0.4411428571428571, 0.025682182985553772], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "In order to make important business decisions and supervise management appropriately, the Board of Directors should consist of diverse members with a balance of abilities. The knowledge, experience, and abilities of members of the Board of Directors are consolidated and disclosed in a skills matrix.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 13, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.03257142857142857, 0.3796147672552167, 0.4411428571428571, 0.02608346709470305], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "The Executive Appointment Meeting, which comprises a majority of outside members of the Board of Directors, makes recommendations to the Board of Directors regarding individual candidates for positions as members of the Board of Directors.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 14, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.03257142857142857, 0.4069020866773676, 0.4468571428571428, 0.03932584269662921], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Furthermore, four outside members of the Board of Directors have been appointed in order to adequately reflect the opinions of outside stakeholders in the management decision-making process. All of the outside members of the Board of Directors have been registered as independent directors with the relevant financial instrument exchanges.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 15, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.03257142857142857, 0.4474317817014446, 0.4422857142857142, 0.025280898876404494], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "In the appointment of outside members of the Board of Directors designated as independent directors, the Company considers both the requirements for outside directors stipulated in the Companies Act of Japan and the independence standards put forth by the relevant financial instrument exchanges.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 16, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.03257142857142857, 0.47391653290529695, 0.4468571428571428, 0.025682182985553772], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Outside members of the Board of Directors provide advice in Toyota's management decision-making process from a standpoint independent of operational execution based on their broad experience and insight.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 17, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.03257142857142857, 0.5008025682182985, 0.4411428571428571, 0.025280898876404494], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "The following measures are implemented to better incorporate the insight of outside members of the Board of Directors and outside Audit & Supervisory Board members.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 18, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "list-item"}, "bbox": [0.03257142857142857, 0.5272873194221508, 0.4422857142857142, 0.02608346709470305], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "1. Review of the criteria for the submission of proposals to the Board of Directors as needed to regulate the inflow of proposals submitted in order to secure sufficient time to discuss each proposal", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 19, "source_label": "list-item"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "list-item"}, "bbox": [0.03257142857142857, 0.5545746388443018, 0.2917142857142857, 0.012439807383627608], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "2. Provision of explanations of proposals in advance to facilitate better understanding of their background", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 20, "source_label": "list-item"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "list-item"}, "bbox": [0.03257142857142857, 0.5682182985553772, 0.4468571428571428, 0.025682182985553772], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "3. Provision of regular opportunities, outside Board of Directors' meetings, for two-way communication between members of the executive team with outside members of the Board of Directors and outside Audit & Supervisory Board members on important management issues and medium- to long-term issues", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 21, "source_label": "list-item"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.03257142857142857, 0.6079454253611557, 0.4468571428571428, 0.039727126805778494], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "To facilitate active discussions at Board of Directors' meetings, Toyota reduced the number of meeting participants (members of the Board of Directors and Audit & Supervisory Board members) from 34 in 2010 to 15 in 2020. As a result, opportunities for each participant to speak at Board of Directors' meetings have increased, and outside members of the Board of Directors and outside Audit & Supervisory Board members are able to voice opinions on almost all proposals.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 22, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"title_level": "section-header"}, "bbox": [0.03257142857142857, 0.6569020866773676, 0.21428571428571427, 0.014847512038523275], "canonical_class": "Section", "content": {"text": "Members of the Board of Directors (As of June 2024)", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 23, "source_label": "section-header"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.03257142857142857, 0.6733547351524879, 0.4468571428571428, 0.2676565008025682], "canonical_class": "Table", "content": {"html": "
