{"attributes": {"furniture": "page-footer"}, "bbox": [0.06606128971751143, 0.9460061571074695, 0.7483411612161776, 0.014389648437499984], "canonical_class": "Page-footer", "content": {"text": "Para consultar as especialidades habilitadas em cada uma das modalidades de atendimento (H/P.S/M/A/HDIA), acesse: bradescosaude.com.br > Busca de referenciados", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 5, "source_label": "footer"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "caption"}, "bbox": [0.1652289264958079, 0.05794739886028011, 0.6687317489996188, 0.012838287353515625], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Table 13 - Scale G2 versus Population Improvement and Resulting IAM 2012 Basic Table, Male", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 0, "source_label": "caption"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.10383014213748094, 0.08685409918064024, 0.39661330060261046, 0.06137299328315549], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "deformation at the sides must be considered. Since the coded letters of the fourth column (CDCLONGLAT) are partially used for the front and the side, the third column (CDCPLANE) must also be used for identification.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 2, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.10375602908250761, 0.15265560987519056, 0.39704618128334607, 0.16008579161108993], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Figure 4 shows the location, and Table 2 shows the center of gravity points in the $X$–$Y$ plane. Since these are one-dimensional lines, the center of gravity is located in the middle of the respective CDC area. The division of the frontal CDC areas is regulated in J224. Thus, the areas L, C, and R are each distributed in thirds over the front. Y and Z are the sums of L + C and R + C, respectively. D extends over the entire width of the vehicle [54]. The centers of gravity shown at the bottom left of the figure are percentages of the vehicle width.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 3, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.10375296988138338, 0.31633006026105187, 0.3964542519173971, 0.07755022746760665], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "The same applies to the lateral position of the centers of gravity. Here, areas P, F, and B are vehicle specific. Since this cannot be considered individually in the evaluation, F and B are assigned 25% of the front and rear vehicle length for all vehicles.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 4, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.10387028391768292, 0.3980287430926067, 0.3966133527057927, 0.062045809117759136], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "To determine the crash center of gravity, assign the relative frequency of MAIS 2+ accidents to the CDC areas. The crash center of gravity can then be calculated using equation [4]:", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 5, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.10367937041491997, 0.5132679711318597, 0.3969205605111471, 0.061310796970274375], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "For the case examined here, this results in a center of gravity of $X_{\\text{CoG}} = 0.12l$ and $Y_{\\text{CoG}} = 0.33b$ for the turning vehicle 68. For the approaching vehicle 69, this results in a center of gravity of $X_{\\text{CoG}} = 0.08l$ and $Y_{\\text{CoG}} = 0.33b$.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 7, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.10379996695169588, 0.5779050947980183, 0.3964333362114138, 0.07820419683689017], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Figure 5 visualizes the collision position found using this method. The vehicles are aligned in such a way that the crash centers of gravity form a line. The line is to be interpreted as a vector of the maximum force direction during the impact.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 8, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"title_level": "paragraph"}, "bbox": [0.10373701142101753, 0.6930288919588414, 0.3476686431140435, 0.010055378239329256], "canonical_class": "Section", "content": {"text": "4.3.2.2 Determination of the further PCM entries", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 9, "source_label": "paragraph_title"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.10345333936737805, 0.7258291253810975, 0.39714224466463416, 0.1927531916920732], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "For the simulation of a crash scenario, the other fields within the PCM must be filled in. Information about the crash participants is sufficiently available within the CISS database. Using the parameters MAKE, MODEL, CURBWEIGHT, and MODELYR, it is possible to determine the vehicle make, the model, as well as its curb weight and year of manufacture. For concrete scenarios, this information is also documented in the CISS crash viewer for each recorded accident. In the statistical evaluation of these parameters within the crash type 68 + 69 with severe personal injuries, it can be seen that vehicles are predominantly from American or Asian manufacturers.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 10, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "caption"}, "bbox": [0.5176538681402438, 0.08822820151724466, 0.3931367306592988, 0.025050182807736278], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Table 2: Relative position of the centers of gravity over the vehicle length $X$ and width $Y$", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 11, "source_label": "caption"}

", "type": "table"}, "ro_index": 12, "source_label": "table"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.5178407250381097, 0.2977438428925305, 0.3969682498094513, 0.16008580649771337], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "German premium manufacturers, in particular, are not to be found here. Furthermore, a generalization and transfer of the results to the German market must be viewed critically due to the higher average vehicle mass of the involved accident vehicles. In contrast to the vehicles in the CISS database, with an average curb weight of 1,811 kg, this was only 1,463 kg in Germany over the last 15 years [55]. Thus, no general transfer of the results and scenarios to German traffic can be made but should be backed up with statistics.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 13, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.5179696431974086, 0.46142354825647863, 0.3961872379954269, 0.1277062392816311], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Finally, the sketch data must be filled within the PCM. The collision position is already determined here. The road conditions are to be identified using the parameters SURFCOND and SURFTYPE. SURFCOND indicates the road condition (dry, wet, snow-covered, etc.) and SURFTYPE the road surface (concrete, asphalt, etc.). The evaluation shows that turning and crossing accidents occur on dry and asphalted roads.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 14, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.5180946312881096, 0.5927369950457317, 0.396439238757622, 0.1429354039634147], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Information on scale sketches of trajectories and end positions, as well as other accident data such as tire tracks, is provided by the TRAJDOC column within the GV data. This variable assigns the individual accidents with a sketched representation of the accident sequence between the accident participants. The sketches contain true-to-scale drawings of the vehicles, road, and surroundings overall crash phases (normal driving to post-crash) and are available via the CISS crash viewer.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 15, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.5181328005907012, 0.8074641530106706, 0.3963736185213415, 0.07847096512957322], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "The procedure shown for configuration J (Figure 5) is illustrated in the appendix for all configurations of interest according to Figure 3. Implementing the center of gravity method for the other configurations leads to the shown center of gravity collision positions.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 17, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.5187336842606708, 0.889577041253811, 0.3956038014481707, 0.028402546208079256], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "For the scenarios for the configurations, D rear-end collision (Figure A1), K left-turn same direction (Figure A2), and L", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 18, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"caption": "true", "picture_type": "image"}, "bbox": [0.0007, 0.0877, 0.185, 0.3746], "canonical_class": "Picture", "content": {"text": "The image shows a portrait of Denise Chase, Director of Unit IV.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 1}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [-0.00048476457595825195, 0.5536769364519817, 0.18581216608605733, 0.2893519555068597], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "We have not finalized the 2 percent fall distribution requests yet. We are waiting on a final dollar number to work with. There is a longterm 2 percent agreement that has expired and the board needs to discuss. I am hoping that this funding can be shared evenly with all units. The outcome of this discussion will decide if we get either $61,000 or $65,000 to distribute in this cycle.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 4, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [-0.0005246326400012505, 0.8453257788681402, 0.1850660948637055, 0.14360440882240857], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "There are so many good projects to consider. We received 12 requests this time, we have seen, in the past, up to 40 applications. Unfortunately, the casino revenues have decreased causing less", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 5, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.2011365937023628, 0.08659026355278202, 0.1863806747808689, 0.2598028862185594], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "2 percent funding for distribution. We have had requests totaling $164,197 to be considered this cycle. Projects are awarded funding based on a number of factors — availability of funds at the end of each cycle, the project merit and potential benefit to tribal as well as governmental communities. I will update you in the next article on projects awarded.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 6, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.20174536728277437, 0.3713153522770579, 0.1795347334698933, 0.21247114972370432], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "You should have received a tribal census survey in the mail by now. Unfortunately, there was not enough thought put into the timeline to allow members in the western end and in other states to receive the snail mail. Households did not receive their surveys until Oct. 25 or later.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 8, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.20134007895865091, 0.5862280273437499, 0.1819092541206174, 0.11386010146722564], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "The problem with that is the surveys needed to be returned by Oct. 25 to be eligible for one of five cash prizes, $2,500, $1,000, $750, $500 and $250.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 9, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.20181703148818597, 0.7051155797446645, 0.1844918302210366, 0.2842348870998476], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "The board liaison was notified by a huge number of tribal members that they did not get their surveys and she contacted the tribal administration office. So then it was decided to extend the return date of survey until Nov. 15. So if you got the survey late and didn’t fill it out because of the expired return date, it’s not too late to send in your survey and you will be eligible for the", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 10, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.4045743318883384, 0.08681202213938642, 0.18904025938452743, 0.44617971280725993], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "cash drawings. Unfortunately, there is probably a large number of households who threw the surveys away because of receiving them late and on or after the date they were supposed to be returned. If you still have them, I urge you to complete and send in. This will help with identifying the needs throughout our tribal membership. An example would be we are always told we don’t have enough children for the expansion of a Head Start program or tribal school into the outlying areas. Make sure your children and grandchildren 18 years of age and younger are enrolled and have their tribal cards.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 11, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.40476357064596036, 0.5386572861089939, 0.18093729158727132, 0.16435529011051828], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "The tribe paid over $130,000 to have the IGA (Innovative Gaming Solutions) come and do an efficiency audit on two areas in the Casinos: 1) Kewadin Casino food operations and 2) Kewadin marketing.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 12, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.40476041468178353, 0.7054602943978658, 0.18598147508574694, 0.2810163395579268], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "They identified in their written action plan priority levels, area of concern, risk and recommendations. The board of directors met with the COO of Kewadin Casinos and reviewed the recommendations by IGA. They gave him a timeline by which he was supposed to report back to board with his action plan. Tony came back with a plan of action, which included a timeline of how he", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 13, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.6084535180068598, 0.0866348564334032, 0.18633247189405489, 0.08927330203172637], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "would implement his changes based on decreased revenue, and to trend staffing based on revenue and seasonal down times.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 14, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.6081196408155488, 0.1812276551781631, 0.18677549780868902, 0.06925351026581555], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "The recommendations being made are administrative decisions by the COO of our casinos.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 15, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.6080730159108232, 0.253321503429878, 0.17684385718368903, 0.08956784132050304], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "The board passed the following FY 2014 Budget Document 002 and Document 003 Resolution on Oct. 31:", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 16, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.6080515791730183, 0.3717264794305482, 0.18857112233231707, 0.18859513118301882], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Be it resolved, that the Board of Directors of the Sault Ste. Marie Tribe of Chippewa Indians hereby approves FY 2014 Budget Document 002 and Document 003 totaling $91,967,061 of which $17,163,452 comes from Tribal Support; and", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 18, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.6077873118330792, 0.5628389981897866, 0.17839456697789635, 0.13924429544588415], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Be it further resolved, that if sequestration results in an additional cut to federal funding levels the Board of Directors shall reduce the federal funding commensurately; and", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 19, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.6078103563262195, 0.705652450933689, 0.1841135313452744, 0.13761355516387197], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Be it further resolved, that on or before Nov. 19, 2013, budget modifications of federal funding levels of at least 5% shall be prepared for review by the Board of Directors; and", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 20, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.6080387766768293, 0.8480465772675304, 0.18488346751143292, 0.1426201052782012], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Be it further resolved, that no new job positions, step increases, cost of living increases, promotions, changes in job title, or changes in job responsibility that would affect wages (except for", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 21, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.8108120355373476, 0.08643784685832698, 0.1885260456364329, 0.21748272407345653], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Head Start, Early Head Start, and the Child Care Center commitments to raise teachers and aides who meet their educational credentials) may be granted except by a budget modification resolution approved by the Board of Directors at a duly called meeting of the Board of Directors:", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 22, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.8114143483231706, 0.3066822479992378, 0.17646835699314026, 0.14337732826791155], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Be it finally resolved, that budget modifications shall automatically be in effect to reflect the rescissions in the foregoing resolve sections and shall be reported to the Board.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 23, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.8116700409679877, 0.4532927127000762, 0.18721036585365852, 0.21678642459032005], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "The board came to a consensus to approve the FY 2014 budget to allow programs to continue their operations. We have been going over budgets line by line and we will continue to make adjustments as needed. My main concern and priority is to keep the core set of services then go from there.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 24, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.8117200004763718, 0.6725490067644816, 0.18559492902057928, 0.11608041158536583], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "We will be starting to organize and plan for our children’s Christmas parties. Any volunteers can call Viola or Rita at 341-6993.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 25, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.8110752905868902, 0.815835588981084, 0.17391299543747843, 0.07076502077501355], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "You can contact me at any time at (906) 322-3819 or dchase@saulttribe.net", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 27, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.02420769668206936, 0.49988316977896347, 0.46708461110184835, 0.021602038871951168], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "This product is a unit of a mutual fund qualifying as an undertaking for collective investment in transferable securities (UCITS).", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 12, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.02402178880645008, 0.5427474752286586, 0.46644684210056214, 0.021526950743140247], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "This sub-fund has no maturity date. However, the management company may decide to close the sub-fund under certain circumstances.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 14, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.023959473400581174, 0.5853510265815549, 0.46761809186237624, 0.021440667873475622], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "The sub-fund aims to generate a long-term capital appreciation and dividends by investing in companies in Asia.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 16, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.023840322727110327, 0.6068367116044209, 0.4676052279588653, 0.05331447694359753], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "In order to achieve its objective, the sub-fund may invest in equity or equity-related securities of companies domiciled in Asia (including but not limited to China, Hong Kong, Taiwan, South Korea, Singapore, India, Indonesia, Malaysia, Thailand, the Philippines, Vietnam, Laos, Cambodia and Myanmar).", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 17, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.024288519882574314, 0.6598762028391768, 0.46652979780987996, 0.010676150438262227], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "The sub-fund will normally not invest in companies domiciled in Japan.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 18, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.0240958478974133, 0.6809195765053354, 0.4673287721959557, 0.053558974847560996], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "The sub-fund invests in particular in companies that in the opinion of the Investment Manager are expected to have a high and sustainable dividend yield supported by sustainable growth in revenues and earnings. The sub-fund is aiming at a diversified portfolio, with no particular focus on an industry or capitalization of companies.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 19, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.024000376259408346, 0.7349238995807927, 0.467391612355302, 0.062411454363567036], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "For its investments in equities or equity-related securities of companies domiciled in China, the sub-fund may invest its assets in China B-Shares, China H-Shares and up to 30% of its net assets in China A-Shares listed on mainland exchanges of Shanghai and Shenzhen. The sub-fund will invest and have direct access to certain eligible China A-Shares via the Stock Connect.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 20, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.024120003304830412, 0.7979973918635671, 0.4670542535549257, 0.020348227896341487], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "The sub-fund may invest in derivative instruments in order to mitigate risks.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 21, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.023991913213962462, 0.8195453029725611, 0.4676306096518912, 0.042160763624237776], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "The sub-fund may invest in any equity-related instrument on any regulated market in any OECD country outside Asia as long as a significant portion of the company’s assets are based in Asia or a significant portion of the revenues are derived from Asia.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 22, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.023636997502024578, 0.8728767506669208, 0.4674700015928688, 0.053347108422256186], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "The sub-fund qualifies as an Article 8 financial product under SFDR (Sustainable Finance Disclosure Regulation). Further information about the ESG characteristics applied is available online at the website of the Investment Manager:", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 23, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.5074764195884147, 0.4894916813548019, 0.4671305735518292, 0.020415664300685993], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Benchmark: The portfolio is actively managed on a discretionary basis without reference to a benchmark.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 25, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.5069397865853659, 0.5426929901867379, 0.46795892482850604, 0.06409054639862802], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "This product is offered to investors who may have basic or no knowledge and experience of investing in financial markets and also experienced investors. An investment in this product is designated to be a medium term investment and the investment horizon should ideally be at least three to five years. Investors should not expect to obtain short-term gains from such investment.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 27, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.5076826886432928, 0.6280064429306403, 0.46657917301829255, 0.01996075886051827], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Depositary: Skandinaviska Enskilda Banken. AB (publ) – Luxembourg Branch", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 29, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.5074447408536585, 0.659735792206555, 0.4675045255335367, 0.02024032964939025], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Dividend income: This class is a capitalisation class meaning that income is reinvested.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 30, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.507004989996189, 0.6915970369664635, 0.4681427448551829, 0.043341868330792725], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Conversion right: The investor has the right to convert his investment in units in one sub-fund for units in the same sub-fund or in another sub-fund. The investor can obtain information about how to convert in the prospectus of the fund.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 31, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.5075330483041159, 0.7448231469131098, 0.4675953934832317, 0.03139052972560976], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Segregation: The assets and liabilities of a sub-fund are segregated pursuant to the law so that the commitments and liabilities of one sub-fund do not affect the other sub-funds.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 32, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.5070082055068598, 0.7871995283917684, 0.4681144602705793, 0.07548405344893297], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Additional information: Additional information about the fund, copies of its prospectus, the latest annual and semi-annual report and the latest prices of units may be obtained free of charge from the management company or on www.fundrock.com/funds/. The prospectus and the periodic reports are prepared for the entire fund and are available in English. The management company may inform you about other languages in which these documents are available.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 33, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.5072146532012195, 0.8727487257050305, 0.4226215939405489, 0.01055443740472569], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "This sub-fund was launched in 2020 and this unit class in 2020.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 34, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.5066535465891768, 0.8936406726371953, 0.4679975705030487, 0.022253417968750005], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "The reference currency of the sub-fund is expressed in SEK. The currency of the unit class is expressed in SEK.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 35, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"furniture": "page-footer"}, "bbox": [0.024380833695574505, 0.9871778534679879, 0.6267475723638767, 0.009838807641006185], "canonical_class": "Page-footer", "content": {"text": "Key Information Document - 22/08/2024 - AGCM Fund - Asia Dividend Fund, RC1 (SEK) - LU2083136650", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 36, "source_label": "footer"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.03569504528510861, 0.12159196807116997, 0.35182494099660416, 0.057232415994521155], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "I am very pleased to present the program for The Attic’s 2013 Sampler Symposium, 5 PM Friday, Jan 18 ~ 4 PM Sunday, Jan 20 (daily schedule 9-4) and I am absolutely thrilled with our faculty. I hope you are as well!", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 3, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "list-item"}, "bbox": [0.03646539083341272, 0.1979440382050305, 0.34627184239829456, 0.027413404511242377], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Lorraine Mootz, Celle, Germany, co-author of Samplers and Designs: Three Centuries of European Samplers", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 5, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"text_role": "list-item"}, "bbox": [0.03626392643626143, 0.25858371641577743, 0.3476808799185404, 0.027271936928353672], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Joanne Martin Lukacher, author of Imitation and Improvement: The Norfolk Sampler Tradition", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 8, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.4165898020674542, 0.1195794082269436, 0.5472830423494665, 0.16369267161299547], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "(Registration is full for this Symposium; waiting list only.) The $450 fee includes a Friday night light supper; continental breakfast, lunch, and breaks Saturday and Sunday; 2 reproduction sampler charts from Lorraine Mootz that are exclusive to workshop attendees; kit for Vickie Jennett’s workshop as well as for Sherri Jones’ Sunday workshop. Registration is limited due to the size of the room. A non-refundable deposit of $50 is due upon registration. The balance will be due upon issuance of the pre-stitching kits for Vickie’s workshop. Announcement of when the kits are ready for mailing will be in a future newsletter. The event will again be held at Hyatt Place Mesa, where they have available an Attic Needlework room block with prices of $119 for King rooms and $129 for Queen/Queen rooms. <u>Here</u> is their Website. Free shuttle service to the shop is provided (airport transportation is not), so renting a car is really not necessary.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 9, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"caption": "true", "picture_type": "image"}, "bbox": [0.026866555562833457, 0.2925028284584604, 0.188580266440787, 0.14976517840129575], "canonical_class": "Picture", "content": {"text": "[The image shows a sampler with multiple alphabets and numbers in red thread. At the bottom, it is signed \"Marguerite Dumas\" and dated \"1895\".]", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 13}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.252329071789253, 0.3013951743521342, 0.5198337164157775, 0.012228021389100582], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Lorraine will present two slide lectures and several charted samplers from her collection:", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 14, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.25158223966272864, 0.31684478015434453, 0.7092277117473323, 0.16362370188643294], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "<u>Sajou, School, and Plain Sewing Samplers</u> Towards the end of the 19th century, the working of fine samplers in many elaborate stitches had given way to the often red alphabet marking samplers made by girls in school during their required needlework instruction. While alphabets and numbers dominated, motifs from the popular accordion-folded pattern booklets were often scattered around the samplers or grouped at the bottom or top. Along with marking in cross and satin stitch, these young hands learned to knit, crochet, darn, sew, patch, and mend with astounding excellence. Lorraine will be showing slides from her collection of early to later alphabet samplers, pattern booklets, instructional manuals for teachers, and plain sewing samplers from France, Germany, England, and the United States. To accompany her lecture she has reproduced Marguerite Dumas' 1895 French school sampler with 4 alphabets, numbers from 1-0, four different borders, as well as some of the popular Sajou motifs from the little pattern booklets so popular around the turn of the century. Also reproduced and available to attendees will be charts of two other French schoolgirl samplers, one from Marguerite's daughter Claudia Morel 1927 and one from F. Potherat 1891.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 15, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"picture_type": "image"}, "bbox": [0.03668544490162919, 0.4430362757240854, 0.19992780359779916, 0.1468981635861281], "canonical_class": "Picture", "content": {"text": "[The image shows a sampler with alphabets, numbers, and various motifs including flowers and a house.]", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 10, "source_label": "image"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.2512662561928353, 0.4827450933689024, 0.7091478896722561, 0.0582992925876524], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "<u>Decorated Towels on Both Sides of the Atlantic</u> This is a slide lecture on European and Pennsylvania German decorated towels from Lorraine’s collection. Discussed is where the towels were made, why they were made, what motifs are found on the various decorated towels as well as their relation to samplers. (An aside: according to Ellen Gehret it is a no-no to say \"show towel\" as that is a relatively new term never used by the Pennsylvania Germans.)", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 16, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.2490937470226753, 0.561303115472561, 0.7140972900390625, 0.19525027391387198], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Vickie LoPiccolo Jennett’s “A Token of Friendship” graced the cover of the Spring 2003 Sampler & Antique Needlework Quarterly. This charming project fits so perfectly with Lorraine’s slide lecture that we asked Vickie to present a workshop on the construction of her “little book.” Here’s what she says about this project: I have always loved letters in all shapes and forms. So, when I stumbled upon a tiny \"pattern book\" in a South Dakota antiques shop, my curiosity was piqued. Exactly 10 years ago I was delving into a subject new to me and to many other 21st-century needleworkers. I had not yet met Lori Mootz, nor been exposed to her wealth of expertise. What I did have, though, was a dogged determination to study and recreate one of these books that blended my two passions: letters and needlework. I took the liberty of creating a pattern; took advantage of several friends (no, I still don't stitch on 40ct linen) and managed to piece together a precious little project that found its way to the cover of Sampler and Antique Needlework Quarterly. You would have thought I made the cover of The Rolling Stone. Fact of the matter, that article opened the door to countless opportunities to write about something I loved instead of just completing writing assignments. I am as delighted today to bring you this project as I was 10 years ago when it appeared in SANQ. If you complete the stitching for A Token of Friendship, you will walk away from this class with your very own completed fold-out book. Stitches are mostly cross stitch with limited long-arm cross, backstitch, and a nun's stitch border. Even if you don't complete the stitching, you can complete the book and add your stitched piece (or the mini-project of your choice.)", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 17, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"picture_type": "image"}, "bbox": [0.05040034596512957, 0.5878219083460366, 0.16921231897865854, 0.1195546517721037], "canonical_class": "Picture", "content": {"text": "[The image shows a small, stitched book with the text: \"A token of friendship and remembrance\".]", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 11, "source_label": "image"}

{"attributes": {"picture_type": "image"}, "bbox": [0.03440585624880907, 0.7016191882621952, 0.19869644165039063, 0.09729051543445123], "canonical_class": "Picture", "content": {"text": "[The image shows a sampler featuring a house, trees, and floral motifs.]", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 12, "source_label": "image"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.24583488650438262, 0.7773307093178354, 0.7190990168873856, 0.10336991472942075], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Joanne Martin Lukacher, author of a new book, Imitation and Improvement: The Norfolk Sampler Tradition, available in February 2013, will present a lecture on a distinctive body of samplers executed by the girls of Norfolk during a dramatic time of social and cultural change in late 18th-century Britain. The motifs and style of these samplers reveal a rich engagement with textile craft and industry that was both regional and international in scope. These works are analyzed for their exquisite beauty and skill and for the civic, moral, and educational values that they embodied in Norfolk society from 1730 to 1830. By closely studying the intricate and distinguishing features of these samplers, Ms. Lukacher opens up remarkable vistas onto a complex social landscape and the significant and primary place of textile art within it.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 18, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.052264943936975984, 0.8089840177210368, 0.1633433383848609, 0.0709073694740853], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "A sneak peek at the stunning sampler design from In the Company of Friends included within Ms. Lukacher’s book.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 19, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.11680888850514481, 0.8948476205221038, 0.7375408637814405, 0.009291754001524311], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Privacy notice: For information about how MSCI collects and uses personal data, please refer to our Privacy Notice at https://www.msci.com/privacy-pledge", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 22, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.09412279826838797, 0.06671074099657012, 0.7715286999213987, 0.06318749404535062], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "We the People of the United States, in Order to form a more perfect Union, establish Justice, ensure domestic Tranquility, provide for the common defence, promtoe the general Welfare, and secure the Blessings of Liberty to ourselves and our Posterity, do ordain and establish this Contstitution for the United States of America.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 0, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.09409028588271724, 0.2379759104658918, 0.78332605129335, 0.03109593833365089], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "All legislative Powers herein granted shall be vested in a Congress of the United States, which shall consist of a Senate and House of Representatives.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 3, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.09445679455268675, 0.3148477396150915, 0.801128130656917, 0.04785900581173777], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "The House of Representatives shall be composed of Members chosen every second Year by the People of the several States, and the Electors in each State shall have the Qualifications requisite for Electors of the most numerous Branch of the State Legislature.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 5, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.09445089200647867, 0.38043558260289634, 0.7981243747618141, 0.04763787990663112], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "No Person shall be a Representative who shall not have attained to the Age of twenty five Years, and been seven Years a Citizen of the United States, and who shall not, when elected, be an Inhabitant of that State in which he shall be chosen.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 6, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.09441590099799925, 0.4465443680926067, 0.8070082724966655, 0.19536981350038105], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Representatives and direct Taxes shall be apportioned among the several States which may be included within this Union, according to their respective Numbers, which shall be determined by adding to the whole Number of free Persons, including those bound to Service for a Term of Years, and excluding Indians not taxed, three fifths of all other Persons. The actual Enumeration shall be made within three Years after the first Meeting of the Congress of the United States, and within every subsequent Term of ten Years, in such Manner as they shall by Law direct. The Number of Representatives shall not exceed one for every thirty Thousand, but each State shall have at Least one Representative; and until such enumeration shall be made, the State of New Hampshire shall be entitled to chuse three, Massachusetts eight, Rhode-Island and Providence Plantations one, Connecticut five, New-York six, New Jersey four, Pennsylvania eight, Delaware one, Maryland six, Virginia ten, North Carolina five, South Carolina five, and Georgia three.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 7, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.09417199855897486, 0.6591232969702745, 0.7719509292230374, 0.030896174733231734], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "When vacancies happen in the Representation from any State, the Executive Authority thereof shall issue Writs of Election to fill such Vacancies.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 8, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.0945456602515244, 0.7080751595846037, 0.7627437237995428, 0.0310050257240853], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "The House of Representatives shall chuse their Speaker and other Officers; and shall have the sole Power of Impeachment.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 9, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.09451505335365855, 0.7844949861852134, 0.7757679711318598, 0.03167605516387198], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "The Senate of the United States shall be composed of two Senators from each State, chosen by the Legislature thereof, for six Years; and each Senator shall have one Vote.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 11, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.09409201273104041, 0.834340224847561, 0.8082856192239901, 0.09659084413109753], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Immediately after they shall be assembled in Consequence of the first Election, they shall be divided as equally as may be into three Classes. The Seats of the Senators of the first Class shall be vacated at the Expiration of the second Year, of the second Class at the Expiration of the fourth Year, and of the third Class at the Expiration of the sixth Year, so that one third may be chosen every second Year; and if Vacancies happen by Resignation, or otherwise, during the Recess of the Legislature of any State, the Executive thereof may", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 12, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.09306052603372714, 0.05681432584436928, 0.7926255091225228, 0.032889504781583465], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "prevention device. We also inspect and test each backflow preventer to make sure that it is providing maximum protection.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 0, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.09279656386956935, 0.1083381913347942, 0.8071145332150343, 0.03038614412633384], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Please call this office at <mark>508-362-6498</mark> should you have any questions regarding cross connections and backflow.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 1, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.0930078348299352, 0.16125821741615856, 0.48535761391244286, 0.015183328768102128], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "For additional information please see the following web sites:\n<u>", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 2, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.09291955715272485, 0.21346786871189025, 0.6888477827862995, 0.015325510908917686], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Massachusetts Department of Environmental Protection Cross Connection Regulations:\n<u>", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 4, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {"title_level": "paragraph"}, "bbox": [0.1269149854706555, 0.3179958138814787, 0.7447748844798018, 0.06690280821265245], "canonical_class": "Section", "content": {"text": "<u>CROSS CONNECTION CONTROL</u>

<u>REDUCED PRESSURE BACKFLOW PREVENTERS AND DOUBLE CHECK VALVE 

ASSEMBLIES</u>

<u>TESTING AND REPAIR POLICY</u>", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 7, "source_label": "paragraph_title"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.0925104355230564, 0.40529758360327744, 0.81229282286109, 0.24198661990282003], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "In accordance with Drinking Water Regulations of MASS 310 CMR 22.22 Sec. 13 (D), all installations of reduced pressure backflow preventer assemblies shall be tested semi-annually by the supplier of water. In addition, double check valve assemblies shall be tested annually by the supplier of water. All tests must be conducted by a certified Backflow Prevention Device tester in accordance with the regulated test procedures. The result of these tests must be recorded on the Standard Inspection and Maintenance Report Form. This form must be completely filled out (including the cross connection ID# from the DEP permit), signed and dated by the owner and the certified tester. All copies of the Inspection and Maintenance Report Forms shall be maintained by the owner. The owner or owner's agent must maintain on the premises a spare parts kit and any special tools required for removal and reassembling of devices which are to be tested. The presence of these materials must be recorded on the Inspection and Maintenance Report Form. Devices failing a test or found defective shall be overhauled, repaired or replaced by a plumber or a mechanical fire sprinkler contractor. They must be licensed by the Commonwealth of Massachusetts and re-inspected within two weeks of the initial inspection date.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 8, "source_label": "text"}

{"attributes": {}, "bbox": [0.09269019894483613, 0.666302579554116, 0.7722480513409871, 0.03381192835365852], "canonical_class": "Text", "content": {"text": "Thank you for your cooperation in protecting the <mark>The Barns</mark>table Fire District water supply. If you desire any additional informa<mark>tion, please call</mark> us at 508-362-6498.", "type": "text"}, "ro_index": 9, "source_label": "text"}
